Single Uncontrolled story ok
Uncontrolled single expansion with mixed string and number item ids.
Shows the default single-expansion contract with a numeric item id initially open. Activating the second trigger closes the first, so use this shape when only one section should be visible.
const SingleUncontrolled = () => <Root defaultValue={[1]}>{items}</Root>;
Multiple story ok
Multiple independently expanded accordion items.
Enables `multiple` and initializes two item ids in `defaultValue`, demonstrating the independent expansion policy used when readers need to compare several panels.
const Multiple = () => <Root defaultValue={[1, 'second']} multiple>{items}</Root>;
Read Only story ok
Immutable controlled expansion with one disabled item.
Supplies immutable controlled `value` with `readOnly`, proving triggers remain inspectable without changing expansion and that an item-level `disabled` trigger stays unavailable.
const ReadOnly = () => <Root readOnly value={['second']}>{items}</Root>;

./src/primitives/Accordion/Accordion.mdx
A coordinated group of keyed disclosures with single or multiple expansion and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, and disabled states.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Accordion.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A coordinated group of keyed disclosures with single or multiple expansion and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, and disabled states."
/>

# Accordion

`Accordion` organises related sections into labelled panels whose expansion is coordinated by one root.

## Import

```tsx
import { Accordion } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it to reduce the initial length of supporting content while keeping several related headings visible. Use `Disclosure` for one independent section, `Tabs` when exactly one peer view should be active, and ordinary headings when all content should remain visible or searchable at once.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Accordion } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Accordion.Root defaultValue={['delivery']}>
  <Accordion.Item id="delivery">
    <Accordion.Trigger>Delivery</Accordion.Trigger>
    <Accordion.Panel>Delivery details</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
  <Accordion.Item id="returns">
    <Accordion.Trigger>Returns</Accordion.Trigger>
    <Accordion.Panel>Returns details</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.SingleUncontrolled} />

## Anatomy

| Part                | Purpose                                                       |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Accordion.Root`    | Owns expansion keys and the single/multiple expansion policy. |
| `Accordion.Item`    | One stable keyed disclosure.                                  |
| `Accordion.Trigger` | Heading button that toggles its item.                         |
| `Accordion.Panel`   | Collapsible content associated with the trigger.              |

`Trigger` must precede its associated `Panel` inside each item. `headingLevel` defaults to `3`; choose the level that preserves the page heading hierarchy. A panel defaults to group semantics and may use `role="region"` when it is important enough to be a landmark.

## Expansion and states

`value` and `defaultValue` are arrays of complete `string | number` item ids. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled expansion, `value` with `onChange` for controlled expansion, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable expansion. `onChange` receives the complete next array. `multiple` defaults to `false`; when true, items expand independently.

Root `disabled` disables every trigger; item `disabled` disables only one. Disabled open content remains readable. Accordion has no loading, invalid, empty, or error state: render feedback inside a panel, or use `StatePanel` instead of an empty accordion.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and styling

Parts accept `className`, relevant semantic attributes, and refs to their rendered elements. The Trigger supports relevant native button attributes; Breeze owns click, disabled, and inline style behaviour.

## Keyboard, accessibility, internationalisation, and mistakes

Tab visits each enabled trigger; Enter or Space toggles it. Focus does not rove with arrow keys. The trigger exposes expanded state and its panel relationship. Keep trigger text meaningful when heard alone, avoid putting controls inside it, and let translated headings wrap. Logical text alignment follows direction; ids remain stable.

Do not use array positions as ids, combine `value` with `defaultValue`, use `multiple` when content order implies a single choice, or hide essential primary content solely to shorten a page. Related components: `Disclosure`, `Tabs`, `Stepper`, and `NavigationList`.
Item 4 prop types

Explicit Decision story ok
Wide alert-dialog decision anatomy and focus lifecycle.
Composes the required title, consequence description, grouped cancel and destructive close actions, then verifies initial focus, wide-screen end alignment, dismissal, and trigger focus restoration.
const ExplicitDecision = () => <Root>(<>
      <AlertDialog.Trigger variant="danger">
        Delete saved view
      </AlertDialog.Trigger>
      <AlertDialog.Content>
        <AlertDialog.Title>Delete this saved view?</AlertDialog.Title>
        <AlertDialog.Description>
          This action permanently removes the saved view and cannot be undone.
        </AlertDialog.Description>
        <AlertDialog.Actions>
          <AlertDialog.Close appearance="outline" autoFocus>
            Cancel
          </AlertDialog.Close>
          <AlertDialog.Close variant="danger">
            Delete permanently
          </AlertDialog.Close>
        </AlertDialog.Actions>
      </AlertDialog.Content>
    </>)</Root>;
Explicit Decision Compact story ok
Compact full-width alert-dialog action ordering.
Reuses the explicit destructive decision at the compact viewport, proving both actions become full width while visual order keeps the destructive choice above cancel and keyboard order remains logical.
const ExplicitDecisionCompact = () => <Root />;
Keyboard Dismiss Disabled story ok
Explicit-only dismissal with Escape disabled.
Sets `keyboardDismissDisabled` on Content for a protected interruption that can close only through its explicit Continue action, while verifying focus starts on that action and returns to the trigger.
const KeyboardDismissDisabled = () => <Root>(<>
      <AlertDialog.Trigger>Open protected alert</AlertDialog.Trigger>
      <AlertDialog.Content keyboardDismissDisabled>
        <AlertDialog.Title>Resolve the interruption</AlertDialog.Title>
        <AlertDialog.Description>
          Escape is disabled; choose the explicit action.
        </AlertDialog.Description>
        <AlertDialog.Actions>
          <AlertDialog.Close autoFocus>Continue</AlertDialog.Close>
        </AlertDialog.Actions>
      </AlertDialog.Content>
    </>)</Root>;
Keyboard Dismiss Disabled Compact story ok
Compact explicit-only alert-dialog dismissal.
Exercises the Escape-disabled protected interruption at compact width, confirming its single explicit action expands to the available width without weakening focus restoration.
const KeyboardDismissDisabledCompact = () => <Root />;

./src/primitives/AlertDialog/AlertDialog.mdx
A modal alert requiring an explicit, clearly labelled decision with protected outside dismissal, contained focus, and responsive action placement.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './AlertDialog.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A modal alert requiring an explicit, clearly labelled decision with protected outside dismissal, contained focus, and responsive action placement."
/>

# AlertDialog

`AlertDialog` interrupts the user with a consequential decision that requires an explicit response.

## Import

```tsx
import { AlertDialog } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it before an irreversible or materially risky action, or when an interruption cannot be ignored. Use `Dialog` for ordinary modal tasks, `ConfirmationDialog` for the standard composed confirmation pattern, `Alert` for inline feedback, and `Toast` for non-blocking status.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { AlertDialog } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<AlertDialog.Root>
  <AlertDialog.Trigger variant="danger">Delete item</AlertDialog.Trigger>
  <AlertDialog.Content>
    <AlertDialog.Title>Delete this item?</AlertDialog.Title>
    <AlertDialog.Description>
      This action cannot be undone.
    </AlertDialog.Description>
    <AlertDialog.Actions>
      <AlertDialog.Close appearance="outline" autoFocus>
        Cancel
      </AlertDialog.Close>
      <AlertDialog.Close variant="danger" onAction={deleteItem}>
        Delete permanently
      </AlertDialog.Close>
    </AlertDialog.Actions>
  </AlertDialog.Content>
</AlertDialog.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.ExplicitDecision} />

## Anatomy

| Part                      | Purpose                                                        |
| ------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `AlertDialog.Root`        | Coordinates open state and trigger focus.                      |
| `AlertDialog.Trigger`     | Opens the alert dialog.                                        |
| `AlertDialog.Content`     | Non-outside-dismissible `alertdialog` surface and focus scope. |
| `AlertDialog.Title`       | Required concise decision heading and accessible name.         |
| `AlertDialog.Description` | Required consequence explanation and accessible description.   |
| `AlertDialog.Actions`     | Responsive group for explicit choices.                         |
| `AlertDialog.Close`       | Runs an optional action and closes the alert dialog.           |

Put every decision in `Actions`. On compact layouts actions fill the width and visual order places the destructive choice above cancel while DOM and keyboard order remain logical. Put the safest initial focus on Cancel with `autoFocus`; do not automatically focus a destructive action.

## State and dismissal

Use `defaultOpen`, controlled `open` with `onOpenChange`, or immutable `open` with `readOnly`. `onOpenChange` receives the complete next Boolean. Outside pointer interaction never dismisses AlertDialog. Escape dismisses by default; `keyboardDismissDisabled` removes that path, so an enabled explicit action is mandatory.

Loading, disabled, invalid, empty, and error behaviour belongs to the decision actions or content. A loading destructive action should remain clearly labelled and must not leave the dialog with no usable cancellation path.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and callback semantics

Use `onAction`, not a DOM click callback. `Actions` supports relevant `div` attributes; title, description, and button parts support their relevant native attributes. Breeze owns the alert-dialog role, focus scope, and inline styling.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and mistakes

Trigger activation opens the alert and focus stays contained with Tab and Shift+Tab. Escape closes unless explicitly disabled; an explicit Close restores trigger focus. State the action and consequence in plain language, use specific action labels rather than “Yes” and “No”, and never rely on danger colour alone. Translate all decision copy together and allow wrapping.

Do not use AlertDialog for success messages, routine acknowledgements, or long forms; do not make outside click the only cancellation path; do not omit Title or Description. Related components: `Dialog`, `ConfirmationDialog`, `Alert`, and `Toast`.
Actions 2 prop types

Navigable breadcrumb ancestors, long content, and current-page semantics.

const RoutingAndExtremes = () => (
  <Breadcrumbs.Root aria-label="Breadcrumb">
    <Breadcrumbs.Item href="/" id="home">
      Home
    </Breadcrumbs.Item>
    <Breadcrumbs.Item href="/very-long" id={2}>
      A very long intermediate hierarchy label that wraps safely
    </Breadcrumbs.Item>
    <Breadcrumbs.Item id="current">Current page</Breadcrumbs.Item>
  </Breadcrumbs.Root>
);

./src/primitives/Breadcrumbs/Breadcrumbs.mdx
An ordered, labelled hierarchy of navigable ancestors ending in the current page.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Breadcrumbs.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="An ordered, labelled hierarchy of navigable ancestors ending in the current page."
/>

# Breadcrumbs

`Breadcrumbs` shows a user's position in a hierarchy and provides links back to its ancestors.

## Import

```tsx
import { Breadcrumbs } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use breadcrumbs for pages nested several levels deep when the hierarchy helps users orient themselves. Breadcrumbs supplement rather than replace primary navigation.

## When not to use

Prefer `NavigationList` for persistent destinations, `Tabs` for switching peer views, and a simple `Link` back to the previous step for a short linear task.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Breadcrumbs } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Breadcrumbs.Root aria-label="Breadcrumb">
  <Breadcrumbs.Item id="home" href="/">
    Home
  </Breadcrumbs.Item>
  <Breadcrumbs.Item id="settings" href="/settings">
    Settings
  </Breadcrumbs.Item>
  <Breadcrumbs.Item id="profile">Profile</Breadcrumbs.Item>
</Breadcrumbs.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.RoutingAndExtremes} />

## Anatomy and routing

| Part               | Purpose                                                                   |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Breadcrumbs.Root` | Renders the semantic ordered trail and applies group-wide disabled state. |
| `Breadcrumbs.Item` | Renders one keyed ancestor link or the non-link current location.         |

Give `Root` an accessible name such as `aria-label="Breadcrumb"`. Every `Item` requires a stable `id: string | number`. An item with `href` is a link; omit `href` only from the final current item, which receives `aria-current="page"`.

Local `/path` links use the optional `BreezeProvider` router adapter. External URLs and native anchor behaviour remain browser-owned. `disabled` on `Root` disables navigable crumbs; `disabled` on an item disables only that link. Breadcrumbs have no controlled, read-only, loading, invalid, empty, or error state. Do not render an empty trail.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and styling

Relevant ordered-list and list-item attributes, including `aria-*` and `data-*`, are supported. Inline `style` and conflicting collection identifiers are intentionally excluded.

## Accessibility, internationalisation, and limitations

Links use ordinary Tab navigation and Enter activation. Keep labels short but meaningful outside the trail, preserve hierarchy order, and never make the current item a link to itself. Long translated labels wrap; do not truncate the only identifying text. Separators are visual and generated by Breeze. Logical layout follows the provider direction.

Common mistakes include omitting the root label, supplying `href` on the current page, using array positions as persistent ids, and using breadcrumbs for workflow progress. Related components: use `Stepper` for progress, `NavigationList` for primary navigation, and `Link` for a standalone route.
Item 5 prop types

 */

 * Destination URL. Omit for the current location.
 */
href?: string

/**
 * Stable string or number crumb key.
 */
id: CollectionKey

/**
 * Ref to the rendered list item.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLLIElement>

 */

/**

 * Initial uncontrolled date, or `null` when empty.
 */
defaultValue?: string | null

/**
 * Prevents focus and selection.
 */

/**

/**
 * Earliest selectable date.
 */

/**
 * Called with the next stable date or `null`.
 * Excluded because read-only calendars cannot change.
 */
onChange?: ((value: string | null) => void) | ((value: string | null) => void)

/**
 * Mutable controlled calendars cannot be read-only.
 * Prevents selection changes.
 * Uncontrolled calendars cannot be read-only.
 */
readOnly?: boolean = false

/**
 * Ref to the rendered calendar root.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLDivElement>

/**
 * Current controlled date, or `null` when empty.
 * Current immutable date, or `null` when empty.
 * Excluded when uncontrolled.
 */
value?: string | null

/**
 * Number of visible months. Defaults to `1`.
 */
visibleMonths?: number = 1
Imports 
import { Calendar } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Keyboard Selection story ok
Uncontrolled keyboard selection in a single-month grid.
Uses an uncontrolled ISO date and verifies full-width seven-column geometry plus ArrowRight and Enter selection, making this the baseline keyboard calendar example.
const KeyboardSelection = () => (
  <Calendar.Root defaultValue="2026-07-13">
    <Calendar.Header>
      <Calendar.PreviousButton />
      <Calendar.Heading />
      <Calendar.NextButton />
    </Calendar.Header>
    <Calendar.Grid />
  </Calendar.Root>
);
Controlled And Read Only story ok
Controlled and immutable read-only date selection.
Places a stateful controlled calendar beside an immutable read-only calendar to distinguish application-owned selection from inspectable selection that cannot change.
const ControlledAndReadOnly = () => (
  <Inline align="stretch" gap="xl" wrap={false}>
    <Controlled />
    <Calendar.Root readOnly value="2026-07-13">
      <Parts />
    </Calendar.Root>
  </Inline>
);
Boundaries Unavailable Invalid story ok
Bounded invalid calendar with one unavailable date.
Combines July date boundaries, an unavailable day callback, and invalid state to show how applications constrain selection while keeping values as stable YYYY-MM-DD strings.
const BoundariesUnavailableInvalid = () => (
  <Calendar.Root
    defaultValue="2026-07-13"
    invalid
    isDateUnavailable={(date) => date === '2026-07-14'}
    maxValue="2026-07-31"
    minValue="2026-07-01"
  >
    <Parts />
  </Calendar.Root>
);
Multiple Months Extreme story ok
Two visible months across a year boundary.
Sets `visibleMonths={2}` around a year boundary so consumers can inspect the supported multi-month layout and date continuity without introducing a separate range-selection contract.
const MultipleMonthsExtreme = () => (
  <Calendar.Root defaultValue="2026-12-31" visibleMonths={2}>
    <Parts />
  </Calendar.Root>
);
Narrow Responsive Grid story ok
Single-month grid fitted to a narrow container.
Constrains the calendar to a 16rem host and verifies the root, header, grid, and seven weekday columns share the available width without changing selection semantics.
const NarrowResponsiveGrid = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control" testId="narrow-calendar-host">
    <Calendar.Root defaultValue="2026-07-13">
      <Parts />
    </Calendar.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);
Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/Calendar/Calendar.mdx
Always-visible locale-aware calendar for selecting one stable YYYY-MM-DD date, with compound navigation and grid parts, date constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Calendar.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Always-visible locale-aware calendar for selecting one stable YYYY-MM-DD date, with compound navigation and grid parts, date constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state."
/>

# Calendar

`Calendar` presents an always-visible, keyboard-navigable month grid for selecting one calendar date.

## Import

```tsx
import { Calendar } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `Calendar` when the month grid should remain visible or is central to the task.

## When not to use

Use `DatePicker` for a compact labelled form control, `DateField` for direct segmented entry, and `RangeCalendar` for an inclusive start and end date. A calendar is not a replacement for a labelled form field when space or form conventions call for one.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Calendar } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Calendar.Root defaultValue="2026-07-20">
  <Calendar.Header>
    <Calendar.PreviousButton />
    <Calendar.Heading />
    <Calendar.NextButton />
  </Calendar.Header>
  <Calendar.Grid />
</Calendar.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.KeyboardSelection} />

## Anatomy

| Part                      | Purpose                                                             |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Calendar.Root`           | Owns selection, visible month, constraints and calendar semantics.  |
| `Calendar.Header`         | Lays out the navigation controls and current-month heading.         |
| `Calendar.PreviousButton` | Moves to the previous calendar page.                                |
| `Calendar.Heading`        | Supplies the locale-formatted visible month heading and grid label. |
| `Calendar.NextButton`     | Moves to the next calendar page.                                    |
| `Calendar.Grid`           | Renders weekday headings and the complete date-cell grid.           |

Keep the navigation parts in `Header` and `Grid` inside `Root`. Breeze owns cells, selected styling and unavailable-date semantics; applications do not render individual days.

## Selection and constraints

The public value is a `YYYY-MM-DD` string such as `'2026-07-20'`, or `null` when empty. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled selection, `value` with `onChange` for controlled selection, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable selection. `onChange` receives the newly selected stable date or `null`.

`minValue` and `maxValue` bound selection. `isDateUnavailable(date)` receives the same stable format and disables a specific date when it returns `true`. `invalid` announces that the current selection is unacceptable but does not render an error message; place application-owned explanatory text next to the calendar. `disabled` prevents focus and selection. `visibleMonths` displays adjacent months but does not change the single-date value model. There is no loading state.

## Keyboard, labelling and locale

- Arrow keys move by day or week according to locale direction. Home and End move within a week; Page Up and Page Down move by month, with the platform modifier moving by a larger period.
- Enter or Space selects the focused available date. The previous and next buttons are separately tabbable and operable with Enter or Space.
- `Heading` gives the grid its accessible visible-period label. Keep it in the composition even if surrounding text also describes the calendar.
- Weekday names, week boundaries, date announcements and month headings follow the `BreezeProvider` locale. Logical navigation and arrow icons adapt to right-to-left direction.
- `weekdayStyle="short"` is the default; use `narrow` only when space is genuinely constrained because one-letter labels can be ambiguous.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not pass date objects or locale-formatted strings, render your own day buttons inside `Grid`, or hide unavailable dates instead of disabling them. Avoid large `visibleMonths` values in narrow layouts. Use `RangeCalendar` for a period, `DatePicker` for a compact popup, and `FormattedDateTime` for read-only prose.

    <Card.Header>
      <Card.Title>Release summary</Card.Title>
      <Card.Description>Updated a few moments ago</Card.Description>
    </Card.Header>

      </Card.Root>

            An intentionally long title that wraps without widening its
            container
          </Card.Title>
        </Card.Header>
        <Card.Body>
          pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis@example.invalid
        </Card.Body>
      </Card.Root>

./src/primitives/Card/Card.mdx
Semantic compound card for grouping one related content summary with optional heading, body, and footer regions.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Card.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Semantic compound card for grouping one related content summary with optional heading, body, and footer regions."
/>

# Card

`Card` groups one self-contained content summary in a semantic article.

```tsx
import { Button, Card } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use a card when a heading, supporting details, and related actions should read as one article.

## When not to use

Use [Surface](?path=/docs/layout-surface--docs) for visual containment without article semantics, [List](?path=/docs/data-display-list--docs) for a sequence, or [GridList](?path=/docs/collections-gridlist--docs) when items are selectable or actionable.

Do not turn an entire card into an improvised clickable container. Put a `Link`, `LinkButton`, or `Button` in the appropriate region, and preserve independent semantics for every action.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { Button, Card, LinkButton } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

interface SummaryCardProps {
  onDismiss: () => void;
}

export function SummaryCard({ onDismiss }: SummaryCardProps) {
  return (
    <Card.Root>
      <Card.Header>
        <Card.Title>Release summary</Card.Title>
        <Card.Description>Updated a few moments ago</Card.Description>
      </Card.Header>
      <Card.Body>Three changes are ready for review.</Card.Body>
      <Card.Footer>
        <LinkButton href="/releases/current">Open details</LinkButton>
        <Button variant="secondary" onAction={onDismiss}>
          Dismiss
        </Button>
      </Card.Footer>
    </Card.Root>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Complete} />

## Anatomy

- `Card.Root` renders the required semantic `article` container.
- `Card.Header` renders a `header` for the title and optional description or controls.
- `Card.Title` renders the canonical `h3` heading.
- `Card.Description` renders supporting title context as a paragraph.
- `Card.Body` renders the primary content `div`.
- `Card.Footer` renders a `footer` for actions or supporting metadata.

`Header`, `Description`, `Body`, and `Footer` are optional. Keep the remaining parts in the order above. A body-only card is valid.

## Accessibility and behaviour

Cards add no keyboard interaction, selection, loading, disabled, invalid, or error state. Interactive descendants retain their own keyboard behaviour. Ensure heading levels remain logical in the surrounding page: `Card.Title` is always `h3`, so use a different semantic composition when that level is wrong. Translate all application-owned text; logical spacing supports left-to-right and right-to-left direction.

Every part supports the relevant native element attributes, including `className`, ARIA and data attributes, events, and a typed `ref`; inline `style` is intentionally excluded.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not omit a useful visible title merely to simplify the surface.
- Do not nest unrelated subjects in one card.
- Do not put footer actions outside `Card.Footer` when they belong to the article.
- Do not rely on colour or the border alone to communicate state.

## Related components

[Surface](?path=/docs/layout-surface--docs) provides non-semantic containment. [MetricCard](?path=/docs/patterns-data-metriccard--docs) presents a named measurement. [Alert](?path=/docs/feedback-alert--docs) communicates persistent feedback.

Component: src/primitives/Checkbox/Checkbox.tsx::Root
Props:
/**
 * Compound control, description, and error parts.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Initial selected state. Defaults to `false`.
 */
defaultSelected?: boolean

/**
 * Prevents focus and selection changes. Defaults to `false`.
 */
disabled?: boolean = false

/**
 * Associates the hidden native checkbox with an external form.
 */
form?: string

/**
 * Renders a mixed-state checkbox. Defaults to `false`.
 */
indeterminate?: boolean = false

/**
 * Ref to the hidden native checkbox used for focus and form integration.
 */
inputRef?: RefObject<HTMLInputElement | null>

/**
 * Exposes invalid state to assistive technology and error styling. Defaults to `false`.
 */
invalid?: boolean = false

/**
 * Native form field name.
 */
name?: string

/**
 * Called with the next selected state.
 */
onChange?: ((selected: boolean) => void) | ((selected: boolean) => void)

/**
 * Marks a controlled selection as intentionally immutable.
 */
readOnly?: boolean = false

/**
 * Ref to the rendered field container.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLDivElement>

/**
 * Marks the checkbox as required. Defaults to `false`.
 */
required?: boolean = false

/**
 * Current selected state.
 * Current immutable selected state.
 */
selected?: boolean

/**
 * Native submitted value when selected. Defaults to `on`.
 */
value?: string

import { Checkbox } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Anatomy And Uncontrolled story ok
Uncontrolled checkbox anatomy with Space activation.
Composes every ordinary checkbox part, then uses Space to select the uncontrolled native input and verifies its persistent description remains programmatically associated.
const AnatomyAndUncontrolled = () => (
  <Checkbox.Root name="notifications" value="email">
    <Checkbox.Control>
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
      <Checkbox.Label>Email notifications</Checkbox.Label>
    </Checkbox.Control>
    <Checkbox.Description>Receive weekly updates.</Checkbox.Description>
  </Checkbox.Root>
);
Controlled story ok
Application-controlled Boolean selection and status text.
Keeps `selected` in application state and reflects the current Boolean value in associated guidance, demonstrating the mutable controlled checkbox contract.
const Controlled = () => <ControlledExample />;
Read Only And States story ok
Read-only, disabled, mixed, and invalid checkbox states.
Compares immutable selected, disabled, indeterminate, and required invalid checkboxes while varying indicator size only where the supported visual state calls for it.
const ReadOnlyAndStates = () => (
  <Stack gap="compact">
    <Checkbox.Root readOnly selected>
      <CheckboxControl label="Read-only selected" />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    <Checkbox.Root disabled defaultSelected>
      <CheckboxControl label="Disabled" size="sm" />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    <Checkbox.Root indeterminate>
      <CheckboxControl label="Indeterminate" />
    </Checkbox.Root>
    <Checkbox.Root invalid required>
      <CheckboxControl label="Required confirmation" size="lg" />
      <Checkbox.Error>Confirm before continuing.</Checkbox.Error>
    </Checkbox.Root>
  </Stack>
);
Native Form story ok
Selected checkbox with explicit native form metadata.
Supplies form, name, and submitted value to the hidden native checkbox, documenting how an initially selected Breeze checkbox participates in browser form data.
const NativeForm = () => (
  <form id="settings" onSubmit={(event) => event.preventDefault()}>
    <Checkbox.Root
      defaultSelected
      form="settings"
      name="updates"
      value="enabled"
    >
      <CheckboxControl label="Product updates" />
    </Checkbox.Root>
  </form>
);
Content Extreme story ok
Long checkbox copy in a constrained column.
Constrains a long label and description to a narrow column, showing that copy wraps while the checkbox target and accessible description remain intact.
const ContentExtreme = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <Checkbox.Root>
      <CheckboxControl label="A long persistent checkbox label that wraps across several lines without shrinking the interaction target" />
      <Checkbox.Description>
        Detailed guidance remains associated while wrapping in a constrained
        column.
      </Checkbox.Description>
    </Checkbox.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);
Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/Checkbox/Checkbox.mdx
Accessible Boolean or indeterminate selection with a visible label, native checkbox form participation, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, required, and invalid states.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Checkbox.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Accessible Boolean or indeterminate selection with a visible label, native checkbox form participation, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, required, and invalid states."
/>

# Checkbox

`Checkbox` lets a user independently select or clear a Boolean choice and can present an application-owned indeterminate state.

## Import

```tsx
import { Checkbox } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use a checkbox for an independent option or acknowledgement. Use `CheckboxGroup` for several related values, `Switch` for a setting that takes effect immediately, and `RadioGroup` when exactly one option may be selected. Do not use a checkbox as a button or as a substitute for expanding content.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Checkbox } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Checkbox.Root name="updates" value="email">
  <Checkbox.Control>
    <Checkbox.Indicator />
    <Checkbox.Label>Email updates</Checkbox.Label>
  </Checkbox.Control>
  <Checkbox.Description>You can change this later.</Checkbox.Description>
</Checkbox.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.AnatomyAndUncontrolled} />

## Anatomy

| Part                   | Purpose                                                                       |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Checkbox.Root`        | Owns Boolean state, validation, and the hidden native checkbox used by forms. |
| `Checkbox.Control`     | Native label and pointer, touch, and keyboard target.                         |
| `Checkbox.Indicator`   | Decorative selected or indeterminate mark.                                    |
| `Checkbox.Label`       | Persistent visible checkbox name inside `Control`.                            |
| `Checkbox.Description` | Supporting guidance associated with the checkbox.                             |
| `Checkbox.Error`       | Validation message associated while invalid.                                  |

Keep `Indicator` and `Label` inside `Control`; keep all parts inside `Root`. The indicator is automatically hidden from assistive technology because native checkbox state provides the meaning.

## Selection and form behaviour

Use `defaultSelected` for uncontrolled state, `selected` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `selected` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives the next `boolean`. `indeterminate` is a visual and semantic mixed state controlled by the application; it does not add a third submitted value and user activation resolves it to a Boolean selection.

When selected, the hidden native checkbox submits `name=value`; `value` defaults to `'on'`. It submits nothing when unselected. `form` can associate it with an external form. `disabled` prevents focus and selection and excludes native submission. `readOnly` prevents changes while preserving the controlled value. `required` and `invalid` expose validation semantics; render a specific `Error`. Loading is application-owned.

## Keyboard and accessibility

- Tab focuses the checkbox; Space toggles it. Clicking the label toggles it.
- Use a short, affirmative `Label` that makes selected and unselected states understandable.
- Do not communicate state only through the indicator colour or shape.
- Use `Description` for consequences or additional context. For consent, avoid preselecting the checkbox.
- `sm`, `md` (default), and `lg` apply to `Indicator`; preserve a sufficiently large `Control` target.

The label and guidance accept Unicode and inherit surrounding direction. The visual indicator stays before the label in logical reading order.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not put the indicator outside `Control`, attach click handlers to the decorative indicator, treat indeterminate as stored selection, or use `disabled` to represent read-only information. Use `CheckboxGroup` for a named set, `Switch` for an immediate setting, and `RadioGroup` for exclusive selection.

 * Compound label, checkboxes, description, and error parts.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Initial selected checkbox values. Defaults to an empty selection.
 */
defaultSelection?: string[]

/**
 * Prevents focus and selection changes for every checkbox. Defaults to `false`.
 */
disabled?: boolean = false

/**
 * Exposes invalid group state to assistive technology and error styling. Defaults to `false`.
 */

 * Native form field name shared by grouped checkboxes.
 */

/**
 * Called with the next selected checkbox values.
 */

/**
 * Visual option layout. Defaults to `vertical`.
 */
orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"

/**
 * Marks a controlled selection as intentionally immutable.
 */
readOnly?: boolean = false

/**
 * Ref to the rendered group.
 */

/**
 * Marks the group as requiring at least one selection. Defaults to `false`.
 */
required?: boolean = false

/**
 * Current selected checkbox values.
 * Current immutable selected checkbox values.

Imports 
import { CheckboxGroup } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Anatomy And Uncontrolled story ok
Horizontal uncontrolled checkbox group anatomy.
Builds a horizontally wrapped group with a shared label, description, form name, and three uncontrolled Checkbox options, then verifies selection and group association.
const AnatomyAndUncontrolled = () => (
  <CheckboxGroup.Root
    defaultSelection={['email']}
    name="channels"
    orientation="horizontal"
  >
    <CheckboxGroup.Label>Notification channels</CheckboxGroup.Label>
    <CheckboxGroup.Description>
      Choose any channels.
    </CheckboxGroup.Description>
    <Checkbox.Root value="email">
      <Checkbox.Control>
        <Checkbox.Indicator />
        <Checkbox.Label>Email</Checkbox.Label>
      </Checkbox.Control>
    </Checkbox.Root>
    <Checkbox.Root value="sms">
      <Checkbox.Control>
        <Checkbox.Indicator />
        <Checkbox.Label>SMS</Checkbox.Label>
      </Checkbox.Control>
    </Checkbox.Root>
    <Checkbox.Root value="push">
      <Checkbox.Control>
        <Checkbox.Indicator />
        <Checkbox.Label>Push</Checkbox.Label>
      </Checkbox.Control>
    </Checkbox.Root>
  </CheckboxGroup.Root>
);
Controlled story ok
Application-controlled checkbox value array.
Stores the selected value array in application state and renders that array as group guidance, demonstrating the mutable controlled multiple-selection contract.
const Controlled = () => <ControlledExample />;
Read Only And States story ok
Read-only, disabled, and invalid checkbox groups.
Compares an immutable selected group, a wholly disabled group, and a required invalid group with associated error text so each group-level state remains explicit.
const ReadOnlyAndStates = () => (
  <Stack gap="xl">
    <CheckboxGroup.Root readOnly selection={['email']}>
      <CheckboxGroup.Label>Read-only channels</CheckboxGroup.Label>
      <Option label="Email" value="email" />
      <Option label="SMS" value="sms" />
    </CheckboxGroup.Root>
    <CheckboxGroup.Root disabled defaultSelection={['push']}>
      <CheckboxGroup.Label>Disabled group</CheckboxGroup.Label>
      <Option label="Push" value="push" />
    </CheckboxGroup.Root>
    <CheckboxGroup.Root invalid required>
      <CheckboxGroup.Label>Required group</CheckboxGroup.Label>
      <Option label="Email" value="email" />
      <CheckboxGroup.Error>Select at least one channel.</CheckboxGroup.Error>
    </CheckboxGroup.Root>
  </Stack>
);
Vertical Keyboard story ok
Vertical checkbox group with native keyboard operation.
Uses the default vertical arrangement and verifies Tab reaches the first native checkbox and Space selects it without group-specific arrow-key navigation.
const VerticalKeyboard = () => (
  <CheckboxGroup.Root orientation="vertical">
    <CheckboxGroup.Label>Vertical options</CheckboxGroup.Label>
    <Option label="One" value="one" />
    <Option label="Two" value="two" />
  </CheckboxGroup.Root>
);

Long checkbox-group copy in a narrow layout.
Constricts a detailed group label, shared guidance, and an unusually long option label to show natural wrapping without losing group or option associations.
const ContentExtreme = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <CheckboxGroup.Root>
      <CheckboxGroup.Label>
        A detailed group label that wraps in a constrained layout
      </CheckboxGroup.Label>
      <CheckboxGroup.Description>
        Long guidance remains associated with every option in the group.
      </CheckboxGroup.Description>
      <Option
        label="An unusually long option label that wraps naturally"
        value="long"
      />
      <Option label="Short" value="short" />
    </CheckboxGroup.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);

./src/primitives/CheckboxGroup/CheckboxGroup.mdx
Labelled multiple selection over composed Checkbox items with string-array values, native form naming, orientation, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, required, and invalid states.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './CheckboxGroup.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Labelled multiple selection over composed Checkbox items with string-array values, native form naming, orientation, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, required, and invalid states."
/>

# CheckboxGroup

`CheckboxGroup` coordinates a labelled set of `Checkbox` items where zero, one, or several string values may be selected.

## Import

```tsx
import { Checkbox, CheckboxGroup } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for related multiple-choice options that should all remain visible. Use a standalone `Checkbox` for one independent choice, `RadioGroup` for exactly one value, or `TagGroup` for a compact interactive collection. Do not use it for a long or virtualised collection; consider `ListBox` with multiple selection.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Checkbox, CheckboxGroup } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<CheckboxGroup.Root name="channels" defaultSelection={['email']}>
  <CheckboxGroup.Label>Notification channels</CheckboxGroup.Label>
  <Checkbox.Root value="email">
    <Checkbox.Control>
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
      <Checkbox.Label>Email</Checkbox.Label>
    </Checkbox.Control>
  </Checkbox.Root>
  <Checkbox.Root value="sms">
    <Checkbox.Control>
      <Checkbox.Indicator />
      <Checkbox.Label>Text message</Checkbox.Label>
    </Checkbox.Control>
  </Checkbox.Root>
</CheckboxGroup.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.AnatomyAndUncontrolled} />

## Anatomy and composition

| Part                        | Purpose                                                                     |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `CheckboxGroup.Root`        | Owns group selection, validation, orientation, and shared native form name. |
| `CheckboxGroup.Label`       | Persistent accessible name for the entire set.                              |
| `Checkbox.Root`             | One option; its `value` becomes the group’s string selection value.         |
| `CheckboxGroup.Description` | Group-level supporting guidance.                                            |
| `CheckboxGroup.Error`       | Group-level validation feedback while invalid.                              |

The group does not provide an item part: compose public `Checkbox` parts directly. Put selection state and `name` on `CheckboxGroup.Root`, and give each child `Checkbox.Root` a unique string `value`.

## Selection and state

Use `defaultSelection: string[]` for uncontrolled state, `selection` with `onSelectionChange` for controlled state, or `selection` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onSelectionChange` receives the complete next `string[]`, not a delta or DOM event. The default is an empty array.

`disabled` prevents all option interaction; an individual `Checkbox.Root disabled` disables only that option. `readOnly` preserves focusable values but prevents changes. `required` means at least one option is required. Set `invalid` and render `Error` after validation. Empty option sets and loading are application concerns; do not render an unlabelled empty group.

`orientation` is `vertical` by default and may be `horizontal`. Horizontal layout wraps and keeps group label, description, and error on their own rows. Prefer vertical layout for long labels or more than a few choices.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and direction

- Tab visits each enabled checkbox; Space toggles the focused option. Checkboxes do not use arrow-key roving focus.
- `CheckboxGroup.Label` names the set; every `Checkbox.Label` must still name its option.
- Keep option values stable and distinct from their translated visible labels.
- In right-to-left contexts, layout and wrapping follow the surrounding `dir`; selection values remain the authored strings.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not control each checkbox separately while also controlling the group, use translated labels as persistent values, omit the group label, or expect arrow keys to move between checkboxes. Use `RadioGroup` for one value, `ListBox multiple` for a large selectable collection, and `Fieldset` for several different controls that form a native group.

Prop types (react-docgen-typescript) 17 prop types 
Component: src/primitives/ComboBox/ComboBox.tsx::Root
Props:
/**
 * Requires the committed value to match an option. This is the default.
 * Allows arbitrary text to be committed without selecting an option.
 */
allowsCustomValue?: boolean = false

/**
 * Compound label, input, trigger, popover, options, and guidance parts.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Excluded when input text is controlled.
 * Initial text displayed in the input.
 */
defaultInputValue?: string

/**
 * Excluded when selection is controlled.
 * Initial selected key.
 */
defaultSelection?: CollectionKey

/**
 * Prevents focus, input, and selection. Defaults to `false`.
 */
disabled?: boolean = false

/**
 * Associates the native form value with an external form.
 */
form?: string

/**
 * Current immutable input text.
 * Current immutable free-form input text.
 * Current text displayed in the input.
 * Excluded when input text is uncontrolled.
 */
inputValue?: string

/**
 * Exposes invalid state to the input and validation message. Defaults to `false`.
 */
invalid?: boolean = false

/**
 * Native form field name for the selected key or free-form text.
 */
name?: string

/**
 * Free-form commits are unavailable in selection-only mode.
 * Called with the current text when Enter commits without a highlighted option.
 */
onCommit?: ((value: string) => void)

/**
 * Excluded because read-only input text cannot change.
 * Called when the displayed input text changes.
 */
onInputChange?: ((value: string) => void) | ((value: string) => void)

/**
 * Excluded because read-only selection cannot change.
 * Called when the selected key changes.
 */
onSelectionChange?: ((selection: CollectionKey | null) => void) | ((selection: CollectionKey | null) => void)

/**
 * Temporary text shown when the input is empty.
 */
placeholder?: string

/**
 * Prevents changes to the controlled input and selection.
 * Mutable state cannot be marked read-only.
 */
readOnly?: boolean = false

/**
 * Ref to the rendered field root.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLDivElement>

/**
 * Marks the combobox as required. Defaults to `false`.
 */
required?: boolean = false

/**
 * Current selected key, or `null` when no option is selected.
 * Current suggestion key, or `null` when no option is selected.
 * Excluded when selection is uncontrolled.
 */
selection?: CollectionKey | null
Imports 
import { ComboBox } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Selection Only story ok
selection-only filtering and option commit
Filters a selection-only country collection, opens the option list, and commits an existing option so arbitrary input cannot become the selected value.
const SelectionOnly = () => (
  <ComboBox.Root>
    <ComboBox.Label>Country</ComboBox.Label>
    <ComboBox.Group>
      <ComboBox.Input />
      <ComboBox.Trigger />
    </ComboBox.Group>
    <ComboBox.Popover>
      <ComboBox.ListBox>
        <ComboBox.Item id="gb" textValue="United Kingdom">
          United Kingdom
        </ComboBox.Item>
        <ComboBox.Item id="fr" textValue="France">
          France
        </ComboBox.Item>
      </ComboBox.ListBox>
    </ComboBox.Popover>
  </ComboBox.Root>
);
Free Form Value story ok
free-form text commit without an option
Enables explicit free-form mode and commits typed destination text with Enter when no option is highlighted, without requiring a matching collection item.
const FreeFormValue = () => (
  <ComboBox.Root allowsCustomValue>
    <DestinationParts />
  </ComboBox.Root>
);
Free Form Option Selection story ok
suggestion selection while free-form mode is enabled
Keeps option selection available in free-form mode by navigating suggestions with ArrowDown and committing the highlighted collection item with Enter.
const FreeFormOptionSelection = () => (
  <ComboBox.Root allowsCustomValue>
    <DestinationParts />
  </ComboBox.Root>
);
Controlled Selection And Input story ok
controlled selection keys and input text
Places both the selected key and displayed input text under application control for selection-only and free-form combobox configurations.
const ControlledSelectionAndInput = () => <ControlledModes />;
Async Options story ok
application-supplied asynchronous option states
Simulates application-owned loading, ready, and error results while Breeze announces status content and selects options supplied after the control renders.
const AsyncOptions = () => <AsyncSimulation />;
States story ok
disabled read-only invalid and required states
Compares disabled, immutable selection-only, invalid required, and immutable free-form controls to make supported root states and guidance explicit.
const States = () => (
  <Stack gap="md">
    <ComboBox.Root disabled>
      <OptionParts />
    </ComboBox.Root>
    <ComboBox.Root inputValue="France" readOnly selection="fr">
      <OptionParts />
    </ComboBox.Root>
    <ComboBox.Root invalid required>
      <OptionParts />
      <ComboBox.Description>Choose a supported country.</ComboBox.Description>
      <ComboBox.Error>A country is required.</ComboBox.Error>
    </ComboBox.Root>
    <ComboBox.Root allowsCustomValue inputValue="Anywhere" readOnly>
      <DestinationParts />
    </ComboBox.Root>
  </Stack>
);
Empty Options story ok

Documents the authored empty-content message shown when filtering removes every available country from an otherwise populated option collection.
const EmptyOptions = () => (
  <ComboBox.Root>
    <ComboBox.Label>Country</ComboBox.Label>
    <ComboBox.Group>
      <ComboBox.Input />
      <ComboBox.Trigger />
    </ComboBox.Group>
    <ComboBox.Popover>
      <ComboBox.ListBox emptyContent="No countries">
        <ComboBox.Item id="gb" textValue="United Kingdom">
          United Kingdom
        </ComboBox.Item>
        <ComboBox.Item id="fr" textValue="France">
          France
        </ComboBox.Item>
      </ComboBox.ListBox>
    </ComboBox.Popover>
  </ComboBox.Root>
);

Constrains a long label, placeholder, and wrapping option to a narrow host while preserving the joined input-and-trigger geometry without horizontal overflow.

  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <ComboBox.Root
      allowsCustomValue
      placeholder="Enter a destination anywhere in the world"
    >
      <ComboBox.Label>Long destination search label</ComboBox.Label>

        <ComboBox.Trigger />
      </ComboBox.Group>
      <ComboBox.Popover>
        <ComboBox.ListBox>
          <ComboBox.Item id="long" textValue="Long destination">

          </ComboBox.Item>
        </ComboBox.ListBox>
      </ComboBox.Popover>
    </ComboBox.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);
Sizes And Right To Left story ok
grouped sizes and right-to-left control order
Compares all grouped control sizes and a right-to-left composition, verifying that Group owns part sizing and reverses input-trigger placement correctly.
const SizesAndRightToLeft = () => (
  <Stack gap="md">
    <ComboBox.Root placeholder="Small country">
      <ComboBox.Label>Small country</ComboBox.Label>
      <ComboBox.Group size="sm">
        <ComboBox.Input size="lg" />
        <ComboBox.Trigger size="lg" />
      </ComboBox.Group>
      <ComboBox.Popover>
        <ComboBox.ListBox>
          <ComboBox.Item id="gb" textValue="United Kingdom">
            United Kingdom
          </ComboBox.Item>
          <ComboBox.Item id="fr" textValue="France">
            France
          </ComboBox.Item>
        </ComboBox.ListBox>
      </ComboBox.Popover>
    </ComboBox.Root>
    <ComboBox.Root placeholder="Medium country">
      <ComboBox.Label>Medium country</ComboBox.Label>
      <ComboBox.Group size="md">
        <ComboBox.Input size="lg" />
        <ComboBox.Trigger size="lg" />
      </ComboBox.Group>
      <ComboBox.Popover>
        <ComboBox.ListBox>
          <ComboBox.Item id="gb" textValue="United Kingdom">
            United Kingdom
          </ComboBox.Item>
          <ComboBox.Item id="fr" textValue="France">
            France
          </ComboBox.Item>
        </ComboBox.ListBox>
      </ComboBox.Popover>
    </ComboBox.Root>
    <ComboBox.Root placeholder="Large country">
      <ComboBox.Label>Large country</ComboBox.Label>
      <ComboBox.Group size="lg">
        <ComboBox.Input size="sm" />
        <ComboBox.Trigger size="sm" />
      </ComboBox.Group>
      <ComboBox.Popover>
        <ComboBox.ListBox>
          <ComboBox.Item id="gb" textValue="United Kingdom">
            United Kingdom
          </ComboBox.Item>
          <ComboBox.Item id="fr" textValue="France">
            France
          </ComboBox.Item>
        </ComboBox.ListBox>
      </ComboBox.Popover>
    </ComboBox.Root>
    <div dir="rtl">
      <ComboBox.Root placeholder="Choose a country">
        <ComboBox.Label>RTL country</ComboBox.Label>
        <ComboBox.Group>
          <ComboBox.Input />
          <ComboBox.Trigger />
        </ComboBox.Group>
        <ComboBox.Popover>
          <ComboBox.ListBox>
            <ComboBox.Item id="gb" textValue="United Kingdom">
              United Kingdom
            </ComboBox.Item>
            <ComboBox.Item id="fr" textValue="France">
              France
            </ComboBox.Item>
          </ComboBox.ListBox>
        </ComboBox.Popover>
      </ComboBox.Root>
    </div>
  </Stack>
);
Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/ComboBox/ComboBox.mdx
Editable keyed option selection with explicit selection-only or free-form modes, separately controllable input and selection state, asynchronous status parts, and accessible popup behaviour.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './ComboBox.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Editable keyed option selection with explicit selection-only or free-form modes, separately controllable input and selection state, asynchronous status parts, and accessible popup behaviour."
/>

# ComboBox

`ComboBox` combines editable text with a popup option collection for filtering a known set or explicitly accepting free-form text.

## Import

```tsx
import { ComboBox } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Selection-only mode is the default: the committed value must match an option. Set `allowsCustomValue` only when arbitrary text is a valid domain value.

## When not to use

Prefer `Select` when filtering is unnecessary, `SearchField` when text submits a query rather than selecting a value, and `TextField` when no suggestion collection exists.

## Basic selection-only example

```tsx
import { ComboBox } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<ComboBox.Root name="location">
  <ComboBox.Label>Location</ComboBox.Label>
  <ComboBox.Group>
    <ComboBox.Input placeholder="Choose a location" />
    <ComboBox.Trigger />
  </ComboBox.Group>
  <ComboBox.Popover>
    <ComboBox.ListBox>
      <ComboBox.Item id="north" textValue="North">
        North
      </ComboBox.Item>
      <ComboBox.Item id="south" textValue="South">
        South
      </ComboBox.Item>
    </ComboBox.ListBox>
  </ComboBox.Popover>
</ComboBox.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.SelectionOnly} />

## Anatomy

| Part                    | Purpose                                                                |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ComboBox.Root`         | Owns input text, selected key, mode, validation, and form integration. |
| `ComboBox.Label`        | Persistently names the editable input.                                 |
| `ComboBox.Group`        | Visually joins input and trigger and synchronises size.                |
| `ComboBox.Input`        | Displays editable filter or free-form text.                            |
| `ComboBox.Trigger`      | Opens and closes the option popup; defaults to a chevron.              |
| `ComboBox.Popover`      | Positions popup content relative to the control.                       |
| `ComboBox.ListBox`      | Renders static or typed generic options and empty content.             |
| `ComboBox.Item`         | One option with a stable string or number key and `textValue`.         |
| `ComboBox.Loading`      | Polite status for application-owned asynchronous option loading.       |
| `ComboBox.OptionsError` | Assertive alert for application-owned option loading failure.          |
| `ComboBox.Description`  | Supporting input guidance.                                             |
| `ComboBox.Error`        | Field validation feedback while invalid.                               |

Keep `Input` and `Trigger` inside `Group`, and `ListBox` inside `Popover`. `Loading` and `OptionsError` describe option retrieval; `Error` describes invalid field value.

## Selection, input, and free-form commits

Selection and displayed text are separate state channels. `selection` is a `string | number | null`; `inputValue` is a string. Each may be uncontrolled with `defaultSelection` or `defaultInputValue`, or controlled with its matching callback.

```tsx
<ComboBox.Root
  allowsCustomValue
  inputValue={query}
  onInputChange={setQuery}
  selection={selectedKey}
  onSelectionChange={setSelectedKey}
  onCommit={(value) => saveFreeFormValue(value)}
>
  {/* compound parts */}
</ComboBox.Root>
```

In free-form mode, `onCommit` receives the current input string when Enter is pressed without a highlighted option. Selecting an option still calls `onSelectionChange`. Do not infer the selected record from `inputValue`; use the stable selection key. Read-only state requires controlled input text and selection as appropriate and forbids change callbacks.

Static and dynamic collections follow the Breeze collection contract: dynamic items require stable `id` keys and a child renderer. `emptyContent` defaults to `'No options'`; set it to `null` only when another explicit status part communicates the state. The component does not fetch, debounce, cancel, or cache data.

## States and size

`disabled` prevents input, focus, and selection. `readOnly` preserves immutable controlled text/selection. `invalid`, `required`, `Description`, and `Error` provide field validation. Use `Loading` while options are pending and `OptionsError` when loading fails; keep stale options only if product behaviour makes that explicit.

`Group` applies `sm`, `md` (default), or `lg` to the input and trigger. Standalone `Input` and `Trigger` also accept size, but the containing group takes precedence.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and direction

- Tab focuses the input, then the trigger. Arrow Down opens the popup and moves through options; Escape closes it.
- Enter selects the highlighted option. In free-form mode only, Enter with no active option invokes `onCommit`.
- Typeahead/filtering uses option `textValue`; provide literal plain text for visual or complex option content.
- Always provide `Label`. Loading is announced politely; loading failure is announced assertively. Avoid duplicating those messages in another live region.
- The popup follows surrounding direction. Keep keys locale-independent; translate visible text, empty content, and status messages.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not enable custom values merely to permit temporary filter text, conflate `inputValue` with the selected key, use unstable item IDs, fetch data inside render, or show `OptionsError` for ordinary field validation. Use `Select` for compact known options, `SearchField` for search results, and `ListBox` for persistent collection selection.

 * Prevents focus and date editing. Defaults to `false`.

 */
required?: boolean = false

 * Current immutable date, or `null` when empty.
 * Excluded when the date is uncontrolled.
 */
value?: string | null

Segment Keyboard story ok
keyboard editing of an uncontrolled date segment
Starts with an uncontrolled stable date and verifies ArrowUp increments the focused locale-aware day segment without exposing internal date objects.
const SegmentKeyboard = () => (
  <DateField.Root defaultValue="2026-07-13">
    <DateField.Label>Date</DateField.Label>

);
Controlled And Read Only story ok
controlled and immutable date-field values
Compares application-controlled mutable date state with an immutable controlled value so the two supported ownership contracts are visible together.
const ControlledAndReadOnly = () => (
  <Stack gap="md">
    <Controlled />
    <DateField.Root readOnly value="2026-07-13">
      <DateField.Label>Read only</DateField.Label>
      <DateField.Input />
    </DateField.Root>
  </Stack>
);
Boundaries And Form story ok
constrained invalid date field with form integration
Combines native form naming, required invalid feedback, minimum and maximum dates, and one unavailable date in a single constrained field.
const BoundariesAndForm = () => (
  <DateField.Root
    form="booking"
    invalid
    isDateUnavailable={(date) => date === '2026-07-14'}
    maxValue="2026-07-31"
    minValue="2026-07-01"
    name="date"
    required
  >
    <DateField.Label>Constrained date</DateField.Label>
    <DateField.Input />
    <DateField.Description>July dates only.</DateField.Description>
    <DateField.Error>Select an available date.</DateField.Error>
  </DateField.Root>
);
English Locale And Right To Left story ok
English date segments in right-to-left layout
Uses an English United States locale inside an explicit right-to-left provider and a narrow host to document locale direction and long-label behavior.
const EnglishLocaleAndRightToLeft = () => (
  <BreezeProvider direction="rtl" locale="en-US">
    <StoryConstraint size="compact-control">
      <DateField.Root defaultValue="2026-12-31">
        <DateField.Label>Long appointment date</DateField.Label>
        <DateField.Input />
      </DateField.Root>
    </StoryConstraint>
  </BreezeProvider>
);

./src/primitives/DateField/DateField.mdx
Locale-aware segmented entry for one calendar date, with stable YYYY-MM-DD values, native form participation, validation, constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './DateField.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Locale-aware segmented entry for one calendar date, with stable YYYY-MM-DD values, native form participation, validation, constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state."
/>

# DateField

`DateField` edits one calendar date through locale-ordered day, month and year segments while keeping the application value a stable `YYYY-MM-DD` string.

## Import

```tsx
import { DateField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `DateField` when users should type or precisely adjust a known date. Use `DatePicker` when a calendar is the primary way to choose one date, `Calendar` for an always-visible calendar, `DateRangePicker` for an inclusive period, and `DateTimePicker` for an instant that includes a time and offset. Do not use a date field for free-form date text or timestamps.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { DateField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<DateField.Root
  defaultValue="2026-07-20"
  minValue="2026-07-20"
  name="reviewDate"
>
  <DateField.Label>Review date</DateField.Label>
  <DateField.Input />
  <DateField.Description>
    Enter a date on or after 20 July.
  </DateField.Description>
</DateField.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.SegmentKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                    | Purpose                                                                    |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `DateField.Root`        | Owns date state, constraints, validation and the hidden native date input. |
| `DateField.Label`       | Visible accessible name for the segmented input.                           |
| `DateField.Input`       | Locale-aware editable day, month and year segments.                        |
| `DateField.Description` | Supporting guidance associated with the field.                             |
| `DateField.Error`       | Validation message associated with an invalid field.                       |

Keep every part inside `Root`. Provide a visible `Label`; use `Description` for format-independent guidance and `Error` for a specific correction.

## Values, state and callbacks

Dates are literal Gregorian ISO calendar strings such as `'2026-07-20'`; an empty value is `null`. A date contains no time or time zone. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives the next complete `YYYY-MM-DD` string or `null` when the editable value is cleared.

`minValue`, `maxValue` and the date passed to `isDateUnavailable` use the same format. The callback returning `true` prevents that date from being selected. Constraints do not replace application validation: set `invalid` and render `Error` when submitted data is unacceptable.

With `name`, the hidden native date input participates in form submission; `form` associates it with an external form. `required` supplies required semantics. `disabled` prevents focus and editing. `readOnly` preserves an inspectable controlled value without allowing changes. There is no loading state; disable an enclosing workflow or show adjacent progress without replacing the label.

## Keyboard, accessibility and internationalisation

- Tab enters and leaves the segmented control. Arrow keys move between or increment the locale-ordered segments; numeric typing edits the focused segment, and Backspace or Delete clears editable content.
- Do not put a literal format such as “DD/MM/YYYY” in the label: segment order and spoken instructions follow the `BreezeProvider` BCP 47 locale.
- The visible label is required for understandable segment names. Description and error text are associated automatically.
- `invalid`, `required`, `disabled` and `readOnly` are exposed to assistive technology; do not communicate them only through colour.
- The input follows provider direction, including right-to-left layouts. Keep the public value in `YYYY-MM-DD` order regardless of visual order.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not pass JavaScript `Date`, `CalendarDate`, locale-formatted strings or offset date-times. Do not combine `defaultValue` with `value`, omit `onChange` from a mutable controlled field, or make an uncontrolled field read-only. Use `TimeField` beside this component only when the values are intentionally independent; use `DateTimePicker` when they represent one instant.

Calendar Selection story ok
uncontrolled calendar popup selection
Opens an uncontrolled long-date field, verifies anchored popup geometry and calendar controls, then selects a date and updates the visible trigger text.
const CalendarSelection = () => (
  <DatePicker.Root defaultValue="2026-07-13">
    <DatePicker.Label>Date</DatePicker.Label>
    <DatePicker.Group>
      <DatePicker.Input />
      <DatePicker.Trigger />
    </DatePicker.Group>
    <DatePicker.Popover>
      <DatePicker.Calendar />
    </DatePicker.Popover>
  </DatePicker.Root>
);
Controlled And Read Only story ok
controlled and read-only date picker states
Compares a mutable application-controlled picker with an immutable controlled picker using the same complete label, group, input, trigger, popover, and calendar anatomy.
const ControlledAndReadOnly = () => (
  <Stack gap="md">
    <Controlled />
    <DatePicker.Root readOnly value="2026-07-13">
      <Parts />
    </DatePicker.Root>
  </Stack>
);
Boundaries And Form story ok
bounded invalid date picker with form value
Documents form association, required invalid feedback, month boundaries, and a synchronously unavailable date on one date-picker root.
const BoundariesAndForm = () => (
  <DatePicker.Root
    form="booking"
    invalid
    isDateUnavailable={(date) => date === '2026-07-14'}
    maxValue="2026-07-31"
    minValue="2026-07-01"
    name="date"
    required
  >
    <Parts />
    <DatePicker.Description>July only.</DatePicker.Description>
    <DatePicker.Error>Select an available date.</DatePicker.Error>
  </DatePicker.Root>
);
Disabled Extreme story ok
disabled long-date picker in a narrow host
Constrains a disabled picker with a long localized date to a narrow width, demonstrating non-interactive content wrapping and full-width control geometry.
const DisabledExtreme = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <DatePicker.Root disabled defaultValue="2026-12-31">
      <Parts />
    </DatePicker.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);

./src/primitives/DatePicker/DatePicker.mdx
Full-width labelled date control with a locale-formatted trigger and calendar popover, stable YYYY-MM-DD values, form integration, constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './DatePicker.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Full-width labelled date control with a locale-formatted trigger and calendar popover, stable YYYY-MM-DD values, form integration, constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state."
/>

# DatePicker

`DatePicker` selects one calendar date from a compact full-width control and an accessible calendar popover.

## Import

```tsx
import { DatePicker } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `DatePicker` when a labelled form field should open a calendar and exact keyboard entry is not the primary task.

## When not to use

Use `DateField` for direct segmented entry, `Calendar` for an always-visible grid, `DateRangePicker` for a period, and `DateTimePicker` for an instant with time and offset.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { DatePicker } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<DatePicker.Root defaultValue="2026-07-20" name="reviewDate">
  <DatePicker.Label>Review date</DatePicker.Label>
  <DatePicker.Group>
    <DatePicker.Input />
    <DatePicker.Trigger />
  </DatePicker.Group>
  <DatePicker.Popover>
    <DatePicker.Calendar />
  </DatePicker.Popover>
</DatePicker.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.CalendarSelection} />

## Anatomy

| Part                     | Purpose                                                                                              |
| ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `DatePicker.Root`        | Owns stable date state, form semantics, constraints and overlay coordination.                        |
| `DatePicker.Label`       | Visible accessible name for the complete picker.                                                     |
| `DatePicker.Group`       | Full-width coherent control containing input state and trigger.                                      |
| `DatePicker.Input`       | Hidden date-state input required by the compound picker.                                             |
| `DatePicker.Trigger`     | Displays a locale-formatted long date or the provider’s “select date” message and opens the popover. |
| `DatePicker.Popover`     | Positioned, dismissible calendar overlay; defaults to logical `bottom end`.                          |
| `DatePicker.Calendar`    | Complete month navigation and single-date grid.                                                      |
| `DatePicker.Description` | Supporting guidance associated with the picker.                                                      |
| `DatePicker.Error`       | Validation message associated with invalid state.                                                    |

Keep `Input` and `Trigger` inside `Group`, and `Calendar` inside `Popover`. Every part belongs inside `Root`. The hidden `Input` is structural; do not replace it with a native input or expose it visually.

## Values, state and form behaviour

The value is a stable `YYYY-MM-DD` string such as `'2026-07-20'`; empty state is `null`. It represents a calendar day with no time or zone. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled selection, `value` with `onChange` for controlled selection, or `value` with `readOnly` for an immutable picker. `onChange` receives the selected ISO date or `null`.

`minValue`, `maxValue` and `isDateUnavailable(date)` use `YYYY-MM-DD`. Returning `true` marks a day unavailable. `name` submits the stable value through the native form integration and `form` associates it with an external form. `required`, `invalid`, `disabled` and `readOnly` expose field semantics; pair `invalid` with a specific `Error`. An empty trigger uses the translated provider message. Loading is application-owned.

## Keyboard, overlays and internationalisation

- Tab focuses the trigger; Enter, Space or the platform activation key opens the calendar. Escape dismisses it and returns focus to the trigger.
- In the calendar, arrow keys navigate days, Page Up and Page Down change month, and Enter or Space selects an available date and closes the popover.
- Always render `Label`. `Description` and `Error` are associated with the complete field.
- Trigger text, weekday names, month headings, week layout and announcements follow the provider locale. The stable value never changes format.
- Popover placement uses logical `start` and `end`; direction and navigation icons adapt to right-to-left layouts.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not omit structural parts, make the hidden `Input` visible, pass date objects or localised strings, or use the visual trigger text as stored data. Use `DateField` when users must type dates efficiently, `DateRangePicker` for an inclusive range, and `FormattedDateTime` for read-only output.

import { DatePicker } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
