./src/primitives/GridList/GridList.mdx
Selectable interactive rows with stable keys, embedded controls, sections, actions, responsive grid layout, and optional virtualization.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './GridList.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Selectable interactive rows with stable keys, embedded controls, sections, actions, responsive grid layout, and optional virtualization."
/>

# GridList

`GridList` presents selectable or actionable rows whose content may include independently focusable controls.

```tsx
import { GridList } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `GridList` for rich rows or tiles that need collection focus, typeahead, selection, row actions, or embedded controls. Use [List](?path=/docs/data-display-list--docs) for non-interactive content, [ListBox](?path=/docs/collections-listbox--docs) for choosing simple options, [Table](?path=/docs/collections-table--docs) for aligned columns, and [Card](?path=/docs/data-display-card--docs) for independent articles.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { GridList } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

const records = [
  { id: 'alpha', name: 'Alpha' },
  { id: 'beta', name: 'Beta' },
];

export function RecordList() {
  return (
    <GridList.Root aria-label="Records" items={records}>
      {(record) => (
        <GridList.Item id={record.id} textValue={record.name}>
          {record.name}
        </GridList.Item>
      )}
    </GridList.Root>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.EmbeddedControlsAndSelection} />

## Anatomy

- `GridList.Root` owns grid semantics, focus, typeahead, selection, layout, empty content, and virtualization.
- `GridList.Item` is one keyed row or tile and may expose a row-level action.
- `GridList.Section` groups statically authored items under a stable key.
- `GridList.Header` is the visible heading inside a section.
- `GridList.LoadMore` is a visible loading row and intersection-driven load-more sentinel.

## Collection content and keys

Author static `GridList.Item` children, or pass `items` and a renderer. Dynamic items must include a stable `id` whose value is a `string` or `number`. Every item repeats that stable key in `GridList.Item id`. `textValue` is required for typeahead and an accessible plain-text representation, especially when visible content is structured.

Selection values are `Array<string | number>` or the literal `'all'`. Do not use array positions, translated labels, or transient object identities as keys.

## Selection and actions

Uncontrolled selection uses `defaultSelection` and may observe changes with `onSelectionChange`. Controlled selection requires both `selection` and `onSelectionChange`. Read-only selection requires `selection`, `readOnly`, and no callback. The initial uncontrolled selection is empty.

`multiple` defaults to `false`. `onSelectionChange` receives the complete next selection, not only the changed key. `GridList.Item onAction` receives that item's stable key. Keep embedded buttons and links as real controls; their activation remains separate from row selection and action.

```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import {
  GridList,
  type CollectionSelection,
} from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function ControlledRecords() {
  const [selection, setSelection] = useState<CollectionSelection>(['alpha']);

  return (
    <GridList.Root
      aria-label="Records"
      onSelectionChange={setSelection}
      selection={selection}
    >
      <GridList.Item id="alpha" textValue="Alpha">
        Alpha
      </GridList.Item>
      <GridList.Item id="beta" textValue="Beta">
        Beta
      </GridList.Item>
    </GridList.Root>
  );
}
```

## Layout, empty, disabled, loading, and error states

`layout="stack"` is the default. `layout="grid"` creates auto-fitting tiles while preserving grid-list semantics. `orientation` defaults to `vertical` and sets the primary arrow-key axis; use `horizontal` only when the visual arrangement and expected navigation agree. `disabledKeys` disables matching keys globally; `GridList.Item disabled` disables one item. Disabled rows cannot receive focus, selection, or actions.

`emptyContent` defaults to `No items`; supply translated application copy. `GridList.LoadMore` calls `onLoadMore` when its sentinel approaches the viewport. Set `loading` while the returned request is pending so duplicate requests are suppressed. `offset` defaults to `1` viewport proportion. The collection has no built-in error presentation: replace or accompany it with application-owned feedback and offer a retry action.

## Virtualization

Pass `virtualization` only for collections large enough to justify windowing. Both modes require `viewportHeight` in pixels.

| Mode                   | Required                      | Optional defaults and behaviour                                    |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `{ mode: 'fixed' }`    | `rowHeight`, `viewportHeight` | Uniform authoritative row height; `gap` defaults to `0`.           |
| `{ mode: 'variable' }` | `viewportHeight`              | `estimatedRowHeight` defaults to `48`; rendered rows are measured. |

Both accept `overscan` (default `0` beyond the directional buffer), `loadingRowHeight` (default `48`), and `estimatedSectionHeight`. Grid-list virtualization also accepts `minimumItemWidth` (default `224`) and `maximumColumns` (defaults to available width). Keep keys stable and provide realistic estimates; do not virtualize merely to hide expensive row rendering.

<Canvas of={Stories.VariableVirtualizationAndLoading} />

## Keyboard and accessibility

Give every root a visible label or `aria-label`/`aria-labelledby`. Arrow keys move row focus along `orientation`; Home and End move to collection boundaries, typeahead uses `textValue`, and standard selection keys follow the current single or multiple selection mode. Enter or equivalent activation invokes an actionable row. Tab moves into and out of embedded controls without changing their semantics. Exact platform selection modifiers follow React Aria conventions.

Translate labels, empty/loading copy, section headings, and `textValue`. Logical layout and focus movement account for the inherited direction; do not reverse source order for RTL.

Parts support relevant native `div` attributes, `className`, ARIA and data attributes, events, and typed refs. Click, value, children, and inline-style props owned by the collection API are intentionally excluded where applicable.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not omit `textValue` or use unstable keys.
- Do not mix `selection` with `defaultSelection`, or mark uncontrolled state read-only.
- Do not attach click handlers to row wrappers; use `onAction` or an embedded control.
- Do not leave `loading` false while an asynchronous load-more request is pending.
- Do not use `GridList` for plain prose or tabular comparisons.

## Related components

[ListBox](?path=/docs/collections-listbox--docs), [Table](?path=/docs/collections-table--docs), [List](?path=/docs/data-display-list--docs), and [Card](?path=/docs/data-display-card--docs).
Header 1 prop type
/home/runner/work/platform/platform/packages/breeze-ui/src/primitives/GridList/GridList.tsx
Renders the visible heading for a grid-list section.
import { GridList } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";

 * Ref to the rendered section heading.
 */

Item 6 prop types
/home/runner/work/platform/platform/packages/breeze-ui/src/primitives/GridList/GridList.tsx
Renders one keyed interactive row within a grid list.
import { GridList } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Component: src/primitives/GridList/GridList.tsx::Item (react-docgen-typescript)
/**
 * Visible row content, including any independently focusable controls.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Prevents focus, selection, and actions for this row. Defaults to `false`.
 */
disabled?: boolean = false

/**
 * Stable string or number row key.
 */
id: CollectionKey

/**
 * Called with this row key when its action is invoked.
 */
onAction?: ((key: CollectionKey) => void)

/**
 * Ref to the rendered row.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLDivElement>

/**
 * Plain-text representation used for typeahead and accessibility.
 */

import { InputGroup } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Prefix And Suffix story ok
currency addons around an editable field control
Composes a currency prefix and suffix around an editable text field, then verifies unified focus treatment and the field's native text entry.
const PrefixAndSuffix = () => (
  <TextField.Root defaultValue="100">
    <TextField.Label>Budget</TextField.Label>
    <InputGroup.Root>
      <InputGroup.Addon aria-hidden="true">£</InputGroup.Addon>
      <TextField.Input name="budget" inputMode="decimal" />
      <InputGroup.Addon>GBP</InputGroup.Addon>
    </InputGroup.Root>
    <TextField.Description>
      Submitted as the numeric text value.
    </TextField.Description>
  </TextField.Root>
);
Related Action story ok
input and related action on one unified surface
Places a related Add action beside an email input while preserving one visual perimeter and matching control heights.
const RelatedAction = () => (
  <TextField.Root>
    <TextField.Label>Invitee</TextField.Label>
    <InputGroup.Root>
      <TextField.Input type="email" />
      <Button size="md">Add</Button>
    </InputGroup.Root>
  </TextField.Root>
);
Right To Left Related Action story ok
right-to-left input and related action composition
Repeats the related-action composition in right-to-left direction to show that logical borders, spacing, and action placement reverse correctly.
const RightToLeftRelatedAction = () => (
  <div dir="rtl">
    <TextField.Root>
      <TextField.Label>Invitee</TextField.Label>
      <InputGroup.Root>
        <TextField.Input type="email" />
        <Button size="md">Add</Button>
      </InputGroup.Root>
    </TextField.Root>
  </div>
);
Sizes And States story ok
matched addon sizes with disabled and invalid states
Aligns addon and field sizes while comparing disabled and invalid groups, including the shared disabled surface and invalid perimeter.
const SizesAndStates = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="bounded">
    <Stack gap="lg">
      <TextField.Root disabled defaultValue="disabled">
        <TextField.Label>Small disabled</TextField.Label>
        <InputGroup.Root>
          <InputGroup.Addon size="sm">ID</InputGroup.Addon>
          <TextField.Input size="sm" />
          <InputGroup.Addon size="sm">code</InputGroup.Addon>
        </InputGroup.Root>
      </TextField.Root>
      <TextField.Root invalid>
        <TextField.Label>Large invalid</TextField.Label>
        <InputGroup.Root>
          <InputGroup.Addon size="lg">https://</InputGroup.Addon>
          <TextField.Input size="lg" />
          <InputGroup.Addon size="lg">.example</InputGroup.Addon>
        </InputGroup.Root>
        <TextField.Error>Enter a valid host.</TextField.Error>
      </TextField.Root>
    </Stack>
  </StoryConstraint>
);
Explicit Group Semantics story ok
explicitly labelled semantic input group
Opts into an explicitly named group when the protocol and input must be announced as one composition rather than remaining presentation-only.
const ExplicitGroupSemantics = () => (
  <TextField.Root>
    <TextField.Label>Website</TextField.Label>
    <InputGroup.Root aria-label="Website address with protocol" role="group">
      <InputGroup.Addon>https://</InputGroup.Addon>
      <TextField.Input />
    </InputGroup.Root>
  </TextField.Root>
);

long address content in a constrained input group
Constrains a long address and two addons to a narrow host to demonstrate overflow-safe composition without breaking the shared perimeter.
const ContentExtreme = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <TextField.Root defaultValue="very-long-host-name-that-overflows-the-visible-control.example">
      <TextField.Label>Constrained address</TextField.Label>
      <InputGroup.Root>
        <InputGroup.Addon>https://</InputGroup.Addon>
        <TextField.Input />
        <InputGroup.Addon>.example</InputGroup.Addon>
      </InputGroup.Root>
    </TextField.Root>

);

./src/primitives/InputGroup/InputGroup.mdx
Visual single-surface composition for a Breeze field control with non-interactive prefixes, suffixes, units, or a closely related action.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './InputGroup.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Visual single-surface composition for a Breeze field control with non-interactive prefixes, suffixes, units, or a closely related action."
/>

# InputGroup

`InputGroup` visually joins a field control with non-interactive addons or a directly related action without taking ownership of field state.

## Import

```tsx
import { InputGroup, TextField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for a protocol prefix, unit suffix, currency marker, or a compact action that operates on the adjacent field. Prefer a field’s own group part (`SearchField.Group`, `NumberField.Group`, or `ComboBox.Group`) when that component supplies one. Do not use it merely to lay out unrelated controls; use `Inline`, `Stack`, or `FormActions` instead.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { InputGroup, TextField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<TextField.Root>
  <TextField.Label>Budget</TextField.Label>
  <InputGroup.Root>
    <InputGroup.Addon aria-hidden="true">£</InputGroup.Addon>
    <TextField.Input name="budget" inputMode="decimal" />
    <InputGroup.Addon>GBP</InputGroup.Addon>
  </InputGroup.Root>
</TextField.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.PrefixAndSuffix} />

## Anatomy

| Part               | Purpose                                                                |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `InputGroup.Root`  | Creates one visual perimeter around ordered controls and addons.       |
| `InputGroup.Addon` | Renders a non-interactive prefix or suffix such as a unit or protocol. |

`InputGroup` is visual composition, not field semantics. Keep it inside the owning field root so the field label, disabled state, and invalid state continue to reach the actual control. Author children in logical reading order; the component adapts its borders and spacing for right-to-left layouts.

## Semantics and state

`Root` defaults to `role="presentation"`. Use `role="group"` or `role="region"` only when the composition itself needs an accessible boundary, and then provide `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`. A visual currency symbol that repeats information available in the label or description should be `aria-hidden`; essential units should remain available to assistive technology.

State comes from child controls and their owning field root. `InputGroup` has no value, callback, disabled, read-only, invalid, loading, empty, or error API. Breeze reflects descendant focus, disabled, and invalid data states on the shared perimeter where supported. Do not implement application state on the group.

Match `Addon` size to the adjacent control: `sm`, `md` (default), or `lg`. Mixed sizes create broken alignment and misleading hit areas.

## Keyboard and accessibility

`InputGroup` adds no keyboard behaviour. Focus follows its interactive descendants in DOM order. `Addon` is not interactive and must not contain buttons or links; place a real Breeze button directly in `Root` for a related action. Ensure the field’s visible label names the editable control, and ensure an adjacent icon-only action has its own accessible name.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not add a second accessible label to a presentational group, put interactive content inside `Addon`, mix control sizes, or use CSS to rearrange the authored reading order. Use `SearchField.Group`, `NumberField.Group`, or `ComboBox.Group` for their native compound anatomy; use layout primitives for unrelated controls.

 */

 */

/**

 */
size?: ControlSize

Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/List/List.mdx
Native ordered or unordered content list with deliberately non-interactive semantics.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './List.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Native ordered or unordered content list with deliberately non-interactive semantics."
/>

# List

`List` presents related text or content using native ordered or unordered list semantics.

```tsx
import { List } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `List` for prose-like groups where order either does or does not matter.

## When not to use

Use [DescriptionList](?path=/docs/data-display-descriptionlist--docs) for term-value metadata, [ListBox](?path=/docs/collections-listbox--docs) for option selection, or [GridList](?path=/docs/collections-gridlist--docs) for selectable rows with richer content.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { List } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function ReviewSteps() {
  return (
    <List.Root ordered>
      <List.Item>Prepare the changes</List.Item>
      <List.Item>Review the summary</List.Item>
      <List.Item>Publish the release</List.Item>
    </List.Root>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.OrderedAndUnordered} />

## Anatomy and semantics

- `List.Root` renders `ul` by default and `ol` when `ordered` is `true`.
- `List.Item` renders one native `li`.

List owns no selection, action, focus-management, loading, disabled, invalid, empty, or error behaviour.

## Accessibility and keyboard behaviour

Use `List.Item` children directly inside `List.Root` so native list semantics remain intact. The component has no special keyboard interaction. Native list semantics work in both directions; translate visible content and choose `ordered` only when sequence is meaningful.

Both parts support the relevant native list attributes, including `className`, ARIA and data attributes, events, and a typed `ref`; inline `style` is intentionally excluded.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not use `ordered` only to obtain number markers when order is irrelevant.
- Do not use list items as buttons without rendering a real interactive control inside them.
- Do not use `List` as a selectable collection; it deliberately has no collection value format or callbacks.

## Related components

[DescriptionList](?path=/docs/data-display-descriptionlist--docs), [GridList](?path=/docs/collections-gridlist--docs), and [NavigationList](?path=/docs/primitives-navigation-navigationlist--docs) cover metadata, interactive rows, and navigation respectively.

 */

collections-listbox · ./src/primitives/ListBox/ListBox.stories.tsx
Coordinates collection rendering, focus, typeahead, and semantic selection.
Prop types (react-docgen-typescript) 14 prop types 
Component: src/primitives/ListBox/ListBox.tsx::Root
Props:
/**
 * Renders one compound item for each generic item.
 * Static compound item elements.
 */
children: ((item: Item) => ReactElement<unknown, string | JSXElementConstructor<any>>) | ReactNode

/**
 * Excluded when selection is controlled.
 * Initial selected keys. Defaults to an empty selection.
 */
defaultSelection?: CollectionSelection

/**
 * Keys whose items cannot receive focus, selection, or actions.
 */
disabledKeys?: Iterable<CollectionKey>

/**
 * Content displayed when the collection has no items.
 */
emptyContent?: ReactNode

/**
 * Exposes invalid state to assistive technology and error styling. Defaults to `false`.
 */
invalid?: boolean = false

/**
 * Generic items with stable `id` keys.
 * Excluded when static compound item elements are supplied.
 */
items?: Iterable<Item>

/**
 * Enables multiple selection. Defaults to `false`.
 */
multiple?: boolean = false

/**
 * Called with the next selected keys.
 * Excluded because read-only selection cannot change.
 */
onSelectionChange?: ((selection: CollectionSelection) => void) | ((selection: CollectionSelection) => void)

/**
 * Primary item layout and arrow-key navigation axis. Defaults to `vertical`.
 */
orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" = vertical

/**
 * Controlled mutable state cannot be marked read-only.
 * Marks a controlled selection as intentionally immutable.
 * Uncontrolled state cannot be marked read-only.
 */
readOnly?: boolean = false

/**
 * Ref to the rendered listbox.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLDivElement>

/**
 * Marks the listbox as requiring a selection. Defaults to `false`.
 */
required?: boolean = false

/**
 * Current selected keys.
 * Current immutable selected keys.
 * Excluded when selection is uncontrolled.
 */
selection?: CollectionSelection

/**
 * Optional fixed- or variable-height windowing configuration.
 */
virtualization?: CollectionVirtualization
Imports 
import { ListBox } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";

Uses static keyed options and verifies that typeahead moves focus to the option whose text value matches the typed character.
const StaticKeyboard = () => (
  <ListBox.Root aria-label="People">
    <ListBox.Item id="ada" textValue="Ada">
      Ada
    </ListBox.Item>
    <ListBox.Item id="bob" textValue="Bob">
      Bob
    </ListBox.Item>
  </ListBox.Root>
);
Multiple Selection story ok
statically authored items with multiple selection
Authors stable keyed items directly and enables multiple selection while leaving selected-state ownership with ListBox.
const MultipleSelection = () => (
  <ListBox.Root aria-label="Items" multiple>
    <ListBox.Item id={1} textValue="Alpha">
      Alpha
    </ListBox.Item>
    <ListBox.Item id={2} textValue="Beta">
      Beta
    </ListBox.Item>
  </ListBox.Root>
);

controlled and read-only listbox selection contracts
Compares application-controlled mutable selection with an intentionally immutable selected option so state ownership remains explicit.
const ControlledAndReadOnly = () => (
  <Stack gap="md">
    <Controlled />
    <ListBox.Root aria-label="Read only" readOnly selection={['one']}>
      <ListBox.Item id="one" textValue="One">
        One
      </ListBox.Item>
    </ListBox.Root>
  </Stack>
);

Demonstrates application-owned empty content beside a required invalid collection whose only option is disabled and unavailable for interaction.
const EmptyDisabledInvalid = () => (
  <Stack gap="md">
    <ListBox.Root aria-label="Empty" emptyContent="No matches">
      {null}
    </ListBox.Root>
    <ListBox.Root
      aria-label="Invalid"
      disabledKeys={['off']}
      invalid
      required
    >
      <ListBox.Item id="off" textValue="Unavailable">
        Unavailable
      </ListBox.Item>
    </ListBox.Root>
  </Stack>
);

variable-height listbox virtualization with loading sentinel
Enables variable-height windowing and renders the loading sentinel for an application-owned pagination request at a bounded viewport height.
const VariableVirtualizationAndLoading = () => (
  <ListBox.Root
    aria-label="Virtual options"
    virtualization={{
      estimatedRowHeight: 44,
      mode: 'variable',
      overscan: 72,
      viewportHeight: 132,
    }}
  >
    <ListBox.Item id="one" textValue="One">
      One
    </ListBox.Item>
    <ListBox.Item id="two" textValue="Two with wrapping content">
      Two with wrapping content that demonstrates live row measurement
    </ListBox.Item>
    <ListBox.LoadMore loading onLoadMore={() => undefined}>
      Loading more options
    </ListBox.LoadMore>
  </ListBox.Root>
);

./src/primitives/ListBox/ListBox.mdx
Persistent selectable collection with static or typed items, single or multiple selection, disabled keys, actions, empty state, incremental loading, and fixed or variable virtualization.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './ListBox.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Persistent selectable collection with static or typed items, single or multiple selection, disabled keys, actions, empty state, incremental loading, and fixed or variable virtualization."
/>

# ListBox

`ListBox` presents a persistent collection of options with keyboard focus, typeahead, and optional single or multiple selection.

## Import

```tsx
import { ListBox } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it when options should remain visible, when multiple selection is needed, or when a large collection benefits from virtualisation or incremental loading. Prefer `Select` for compact single selection, `ComboBox` for editable filtering, `RadioGroup` for a short visible one-of-many choice, and `List` for non-interactive content. Do not use listbox options as navigation links or a collection of unrelated buttons.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { ListBox } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<ListBox.Root aria-label="Priority" defaultSelection={['normal']}>
  <ListBox.Item id="low" textValue="Low">
    Low
  </ListBox.Item>
  <ListBox.Item id="normal" textValue="Normal">
    Normal
  </ListBox.Item>
  <ListBox.Item id="high" textValue="High">
    High
  </ListBox.Item>
</ListBox.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.StaticKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part               | Purpose                                                                                                           |
| ------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ListBox.Root`     | Owns collection rendering, focus, typeahead, selection, empty state, orientation, and optional virtualisation.    |
| `ListBox.Item`     | One selectable option with a stable key, plain-text representation, disabled state, and optional semantic action. |
| `ListBox.LoadMore` | Loading row and deduplicated intersection sentinel for application-owned incremental loading.                     |

`Root` must have an accessible name through `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`. Use `onAction` for an option’s semantic action only when that action is consistent with listbox interaction; it receives the item key.

## Static and dynamic collections

Static collections use authored `Item` children. Dynamic collections require items with stable `id: string | number` and a renderer.

```tsx
<ListBox.Root items={people} aria-label="Assignees" multiple>
  {(person) => (
    <ListBox.Item id={person.id} textValue={person.name}>
      {person.name}
    </ListBox.Item>
  )}
</ListBox.Root>
```

Never mix static children with `items`. `textValue` is required for typeahead and accessibility, especially when visible content contains markup. `disabledKeys` disables matching keys without changing the data. Item-level `disabled` is useful for static collections.

## Selection and state

Selections use `CollectionKey[] | 'all'`, where each key is a string or number. Use `defaultSelection` for uncontrolled state, `selection` with `onSelectionChange` for controlled state, or `selection` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onSelectionChange` receives the complete next selection. `multiple` defaults to `false`; even single selection is represented as an array.

`required` adds required semantics; `invalid` adds invalid semantics and styling, but validation messages belong in surrounding form composition. `readOnly` exposes immutable semantics and prevents application state changes. An empty collection displays `emptyContent`, defaulting to `'No options'`. The component has no root loading prop; use `LoadMore` or application-owned status content.

`orientation` defaults to `vertical`; horizontal listboxes should contain a small, naturally horizontal set.

## Virtualisation and loading

Pass `virtualization` only for collections large enough to justify windowing. Fixed mode requires an authoritative `rowHeight`; variable mode measures live rows and uses `estimatedRowHeight` only initially.

```tsx
<ListBox.Root
  items={items}
  aria-label="Results"
  virtualization={{ mode: 'fixed', viewportHeight: 320, rowHeight: 48, gap: 4 }}
>
  {(item) => (
    <ListBox.Item id={item.id} textValue={item.name}>
      {item.name}
    </ListBox.Item>
  )}
</ListBox.Root>
```

Common virtualisation options are `viewportHeight` (required pixels), `gap` (`0`), `overscan` (`0` beyond the engine buffer), `loadingRowHeight` (`48`), and `estimatedSectionHeight`. Variable mode’s `estimatedRowHeight` defaults to `48`.

`LoadMore` calls `onLoadMore` once when its offset threshold is reached and suppresses duplicate calls while `loading` is true. `offset` defaults to `1` viewport proportion. The application owns fetching, errors, retries, end-of-list state, and stable item accumulation.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and direction

- Tab focuses the listbox; arrow keys move the active option along its orientation.
- Typeahead matches `textValue`. Enter or Space selects according to selection mode; selection behaviour follows accessible listbox conventions.
- Disabled options cannot receive focus, selection, or actions.
- Give the root a persistent accessible name. Do not place interactive descendants inside an option.
- Right-to-left horizontal navigation follows platform direction; keys remain stable while labels and empty/loading content may be translated.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not use array indexes as keys, omit the accessible root name, mix links or buttons into options, enable virtualisation without a fixed viewport height, or forget to set `loading` while a load-more request is pending. Use `Select`, `ComboBox`, `RadioGroup`, or non-interactive `List` when their semantics fit better.
Item 6 prop types
/home/runner/work/platform/platform/packages/breeze-ui/src/primitives/ListBox/ListBox.tsx
Renders one keyed option within a Breeze collection.
import { ListBox } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Component: src/primitives/ListBox/ListBox.tsx::Item (react-docgen-typescript)
/**
 * Visible option content.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Prevents focus, selection, and actions for this option. Defaults to `false`.
 */
disabled?: boolean = false

/**
 * Stable string or number option key.
 */
id: CollectionKey

/**
 * Called with this option key when its action is invoked.
 */
onAction?: ((key: CollectionKey) => void)

/**
 * Ref to the rendered option.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLDivElement>

/**
 * Plain-text representation used for typeahead and accessibility.
 */
textValue: string

 */

/**
 * Called once when more consumer-owned items should be requested.
 */

/**

 */

Props:
/**
 * Trigger and popover parts.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Initial popup state. Defaults to `false`.
 */
defaultOpen?: boolean

/**
 * Called with the next popup state.
 */
onOpenChange?: ((open: boolean) => void) | ((open: boolean) => void)

/**
 * Current popup state.
 * Current immutable popup state.
 */
open?: boolean

/**
 * Marks controlled popup state as intentionally immutable.
 */
readOnly?: boolean
Imports

static nested menu keyboard focus lifecycle
Opens a static action menu, enters a nested submenu with ArrowRight, and verifies Escape returns focus and context to the parent menu.
const StaticNestedKeyboardAndTouch = () => (
  <Menu.Root>
    <Menu.Trigger>Actions</Menu.Trigger>
    <Menu.Popover>
      <Menu.List aria-label="Actions">
        <Menu.Item id="edit" textValue="Edit">
          Edit
        </Menu.Item>
        <Menu.Item disabled id={2} textValue="Archive">
          Archive
        </Menu.Item>
        <Menu.Submenu>
          <Menu.Item id="share" textValue="Share">
            Share
          </Menu.Item>
          <Menu.Popover>
            <Menu.List aria-label="Share">
              <Menu.Item id="email" textValue="Email">
                Email
              </Menu.Item>
            </Menu.List>
          </Menu.Popover>
        </Menu.Submenu>
      </Menu.List>
    </Menu.Popover>
  </Menu.Root>
);

statically authored menu items with read-only selection
Authors density choices directly with an intentionally immutable selected key in an initially open menu.
const ReadOnlySelection = () => (
  <Menu.Root defaultOpen>
    <Menu.Trigger>Density</Menu.Trigger>
    <Menu.Popover>
      <Menu.List aria-label="Density" readOnly selection={[1]}>
        <Menu.Item id={1} textValue="Compact">
          Compact
        </Menu.Item>
        <Menu.Item id="comfortable" textValue="Comfortable">
          Comfortable
        </Menu.Item>
      </Menu.List>
    </Menu.Popover>
  </Menu.Root>
);
Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/Menu/Menu.mdx
A transient keyboard-navigable menu for keyed actions, optional selection, destinations, and nested submenus.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Menu.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A transient keyboard-navigable menu for keyed actions, optional selection, destinations, and nested submenus."
/>

# Menu

`Menu` reveals a compact collection of actions or destinations from a trigger.

## Import

```tsx
import { Menu } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for secondary actions that need not remain visible.

## When not to use

Use `Select` for choosing a form value, `NavigationList` for persistent routes, `Popover` for interactive form content, and visible buttons when an action is primary or time-critical.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Menu } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Menu.Root>
  <Menu.Trigger>Actions</Menu.Trigger>
  <Menu.Popover>
    <Menu.List aria-label="Actions">
      <Menu.Item id="rename" textValue="Rename" onAction={rename}>
        Rename
      </Menu.Item>
      <Menu.Item id="archive" textValue="Archive" onAction={archive}>
        Archive
      </Menu.Item>
    </Menu.List>
  </Menu.Popover>
</Menu.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.StaticNestedKeyboardAndTouch} />

## Anatomy

| Part           | Purpose                                               |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `Menu.Root`    | Coordinates open state, trigger focus, and dismissal. |
| `Menu.Trigger` | Button that opens the root menu.                      |
| `Menu.Popover` | Positions and portals a root or nested menu.          |
| `Menu.List`    | Keyboard-navigable static or generic item collection. |
| `Menu.Item`    | One keyed action, selectable item, or destination.    |
| `Menu.Submenu` | Pairs a trigger `Item` with a nested `Popover`.       |

`Submenu` requires exactly two children in order: its trigger `Menu.Item`, then a `Menu.Popover` containing another labelled `Menu.List`.

## Collections, selection, and callbacks

Author `Item` children directly or pass `items`, where every item has `id: string | number`, and render an item from the child function. `textValue` is required for typeahead and accessible representation.

```tsx
<Menu.List items={actions} aria-label="Actions">
  {(action) => (
    <Menu.Item id={action.id} textValue={action.label} onAction={action.run}>
      {action.label}
    </Menu.Item>
  )}
</Menu.List>
```

Selection is off unless `selection` or `defaultSelection` is supplied. Use `defaultSelection` for uncontrolled state, `selection` with `onSelectionChange` for controlled state, or `selection` with `readOnly` for immutable state. Values are `(string | number)[] | 'all'`; `onSelectionChange` receives the complete next value. Set `multiple` for multiple selection. `onAction` receives the activated item's id even when it is also selectable.

Root open state follows the same pattern with `defaultOpen`, or `open` plus `onOpenChange`; `open` plus `readOnly` is immutable. `onOpenChange` receives the complete next Boolean. `disabledKeys` disables matching ids and `Item.disabled` disables one item. Empty content defaults to `No actions`. Loading and load errors are application-owned and should be expressed as deliberate non-action content or adjacent feedback.

## Routing and presentation

An item without `href` is an action. A local `/path` item uses the provider router; other URLs keep native navigation. Do not combine a destination with an unrelated action. `Popover.placement` is `'bottom start'` by default and also accepts `'bottom end'`, `'top start'`, or `'top end'`; `nonModal` defaults to `false`.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and styling

Relevant button, menu, and item `aria-*` and `data-*` attributes are supported. Breeze intentionally owns click, selection, disabled, and inline style behaviour.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and internationalisation

Enter, Space, or Arrow Down opens the menu. Arrow Up and Arrow Down move focus; Home and End reach boundaries; character keys use `textValue`; Enter or Space activates; Escape closes and restores focus. Arrow Right enters a submenu and Arrow Left returns in left-to-right contexts; React Aria mirrors directional behaviour for RTL. Disabled items are skipped.

Label every `List`, including nested lists. Keep items concise and non-interactive inside; do not place controls in an item. Translate labels, `textValue`, and `emptyContent` together while keeping stable ids. Common mistakes are using Menu as a select field, omitting `textValue`, mixing controlled and uncontrolled props, and nesting a submenu without its own label.

Related components: `Popover`, `Select`, `NavigationList`, `Toolbar`, and `UserMenu`.

 */

 */

    <NavigationList.Root aria-label="Vertical navigation">

      </NavigationList.Item>
      <NavigationList.Item href="/team" id={2}>

    <NavigationList.Root
      aria-label="Horizontal navigation"

      </NavigationList.Item>

      </NavigationList.Item>

Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/NavigationList/NavigationList.mdx
A labelled persistent navigation landmark with keyed destinations, current-page state, and horizontal or vertical layout.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './NavigationList.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A labelled persistent navigation landmark with keyed destinations, current-page state, and horizontal or vertical layout."
/>

# NavigationList

`NavigationList` presents a persistent set of application destinations and identifies the current page.

## Import

```tsx
import { NavigationList } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for side navigation or a compact horizontal destination list. Use `Menu` for transient actions, `Tabs` for views of the same resource, and `Breadcrumbs` for hierarchical orientation.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { NavigationList } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<NavigationList.Root aria-label="Settings">
  <NavigationList.Item current href="/settings" id="general">
    General
  </NavigationList.Item>
  <NavigationList.Item href="/settings/security" id="security">
    Security
  </NavigationList.Item>
</NavigationList.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.StatesAndOrientations} />

## Anatomy, state, and routing

| Part                  | Purpose                                                     |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `NavigationList.Root` | Renders a labelled navigation landmark containing the list. |
| `NavigationList.Item` | Renders one keyed router-neutral destination.               |

Set `current` on exactly one destination for `aria-current="page"`. `onAction` receives that item's complete `string | number` id after semantic activation; it receives no DOM event. A disabled item neither navigates nor calls `onAction`.

Local `/path` destinations use the `BreezeProvider` router adapter when present. Without an adapter, and for external URLs, normal anchors are used. There is no selection controller: route state owns `current`. The component has no loading, invalid, read-only, empty, or error presentation; render an appropriate loading or state component instead of an empty landmark.

## Orientation

`vertical` is the default and suits side navigation. `horizontal` wraps at narrow widths and suits a short set. Layout follows logical direction, while destination ids and URLs do not change during localisation.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and styling

Relevant `nav` and list-item `aria-*` and `data-*` attributes are supported. Inline styling and native click handlers are excluded from the public interaction contract.

## Accessibility and common mistakes

Every root needs a distinct accessible name when a page has more than one navigation landmark. Tab visits enabled links and Enter activates them. Use visible, translated link text, allow it to wrap, and do not rely on the current background colour alone. Do not use `onAction` as a substitute for navigation, put buttons inside items, mark several pages current, or use unstable ids.

Related components: `Breadcrumbs` for hierarchy, `Tabs` for peer panels, `Menu` for transient commands, and `NavigationSection` for a composed navigation pattern.

 */

 */
current?: boolean = false

/**
 * Prevents navigation and action. Defaults to `false`.
 */
disabled?: boolean = false

/**
 * Destination URL.
 */
href: string

/**
 * Stable string or number destination key.
 */
id: CollectionKey

/**
 * Called with this destination key after activation.
 */
onAction?: ((key: CollectionKey) => void)

/**
 * Ref to the rendered list item.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLLIElement>

 */
children: ReactNode

 */

/**
 * Prevents editing, stepping, and focus. Defaults to `false`.
 */
disabled?: boolean = false

/**

 * Smallest permitted value.

 */

 * Amount applied by stepper buttons and arrow keys. Defaults to `1`.
 */
step?: number

/**
 * Current number, or `null` for an empty input.
 * Current immutable number, or `null` for an empty input.
 */
value?: number | null

  <NumberField.Root defaultValue={1} max={5} min={0}>
    <NumberField.Label>Quantity</NumberField.Label>
    <NumberField.Group>
      <NumberField.DecrementButton aria-label="Decrease">
        −
      </NumberField.DecrementButton>
      <NumberField.Input name="quantity" />
      <NumberField.IncrementButton aria-label="Increase">
        +
      </NumberField.IncrementButton>
    </NumberField.Group>
    <NumberField.Description>
      Use Arrow Up or Arrow Down, or the step buttons.
    </NumberField.Description>
  </NumberField.Root>
);

const Controlled = () => <ControlledExample />;
Uncontrolled And Locale Formatting story ok
uncontrolled value with locale-aware number formatting
Starts with an uncontrolled decimal value and delegates locale-sensitive grouping and fractional display to the provider locale.

  <NumberField.Root
    defaultValue={1234.5}
    formatOptions={{ maximumFractionDigits: 2 }}
  >
    <NumberField.Label>Uncontrolled amount</NumberField.Label>
    <Parts />
  </NumberField.Root>

Read Only And States story ok
read-only disabled and invalid numeric field states
Compares immutable, disabled, and required invalid numeric fields to expose their distinct interaction, surface, and validation contracts.
const ReadOnlyAndStates = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="bounded">
    <Stack gap="lg">
      <NumberField.Root readOnly value={42}>
        <NumberField.Label>Read-only</NumberField.Label>
        <NumberField.Group>
          <NumberField.Input />
        </NumberField.Group>
      </NumberField.Root>
      <NumberField.Root disabled defaultValue={7}>
        <NumberField.Label>Disabled</NumberField.Label>
        <Parts />
      </NumberField.Root>
      <NumberField.Root
        invalid
        required
        value={null}
        onChange={() => undefined}
      >
        <NumberField.Label>Invalid</NumberField.Label>
        <Parts />
        <NumberField.Error>Enter a number.</NumberField.Error>
      </NumberField.Root>
    </Stack>
  </StoryConstraint>
);

supported input and stepper button compositions
Shows input-only, two-stepper, decrement-only, and increment-only groups so applications can choose only the numeric actions their workflow requires.
const InputAndStepperCompositions = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="bounded">
    <Stack gap="lg">
      <NumberField.Root>
        <NumberField.Label>Input only</NumberField.Label>
        <NumberField.Group>
          <NumberField.Input />
        </NumberField.Group>
      </NumberField.Root>
      <NumberField.Root>
        <NumberField.Label>Both steppers</NumberField.Label>
        <NumberField.Group>
          <NumberField.DecrementButton aria-label="Decrease">
            −
          </NumberField.DecrementButton>
          <NumberField.Input />
          <NumberField.IncrementButton aria-label="Increase">
            +
          </NumberField.IncrementButton>
        </NumberField.Group>
      </NumberField.Root>
      <NumberField.Root>
        <NumberField.Label>Decrement only</NumberField.Label>
        <NumberField.Group>
          <NumberField.DecrementButton aria-label="Decrease">
            −
          </NumberField.DecrementButton>
          <NumberField.Input />
        </NumberField.Group>
      </NumberField.Root>
      <NumberField.Root>
        <NumberField.Label>Increment only</NumberField.Label>
        <NumberField.Group>
          <NumberField.Input />
          <NumberField.IncrementButton aria-label="Increase">
            +
          </NumberField.IncrementButton>
        </NumberField.Group>
      </NumberField.Root>
    </Stack>
  </StoryConstraint>
);

constrained long decimal at its maximum boundary
Places a long bounded decimal in a narrow host and verifies the increment action disables exactly at the configured maximum.

  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <NumberField.Root
      defaultValue={999999999.99}
      formatOptions={{ maximumFractionDigits: 2 }}
      max={999999999.99}

      </NumberField.Description>
    </NumberField.Root>

);

        <SizedParts size="md" />

      </NumberField.Root>

  </StoryConstraint>

./src/primitives/NumberField/NumberField.mdx
Locale-aware numeric entry with optional increment and decrement controls, explicit constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, and validation states.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './NumberField.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Locale-aware numeric entry with optional increment and decrement controls, explicit constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, and validation states."
/>

# NumberField

`NumberField` collects a numeric value with locale-aware display, keyboard stepping, and optional increment and decrement buttons.

## Import

```tsx
import { NumberField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for quantities, percentages, rates, and other values that are mathematically numeric.

## When not to use

Prefer `TextField` with an appropriate `inputMode` for digit-shaped identifiers such as account numbers, telephone numbers, and postcodes. Prefer `Slider` when approximate adjustment within a bounded range is more important than exact entry.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { NumberField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<NumberField.Root defaultValue={2} min={1} max={20} step={1}>
  <NumberField.Label>Quantity</NumberField.Label>
  <NumberField.Group>
    <NumberField.DecrementButton aria-label="Decrease quantity">
      −
    </NumberField.DecrementButton>
    <NumberField.Input name="quantity" />
    <NumberField.IncrementButton aria-label="Increase quantity">
      +
    </NumberField.IncrementButton>
  </NumberField.Group>
  <NumberField.Description>Choose from 1 to 20.</NumberField.Description>
</NumberField.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.AnatomyAndKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                          | Purpose                                                       |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `NumberField.Root`            | Owns numeric value, range, step, formatting, and field state. |
| `NumberField.Label`           | Persistently names the spinbutton.                            |
| `NumberField.Group`           | Visually joins input and optional step controls.              |
| `NumberField.Input`           | Editable locale-aware spinbutton.                             |
| `NumberField.DecrementButton` | Subtracts one configured step.                                |
| `NumberField.IncrementButton` | Adds one configured step.                                     |
| `NumberField.Description`     | Associates range or format guidance.                          |
| `NumberField.Error`           | Associates validation feedback while invalid.                 |

Stepper buttons are optional. When present, give each a literal accessible `aria-label` and match its `size` to the input.

## Numeric value and callbacks

The public value is `number | null`; `null` represents an empty input. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives the parsed next number or `null`, never a DOM event and never `NaN`.

`min`, `max`, and `step` constrain stepping and validity; `step` defaults to `1`. `formatOptions` accepts `Intl.NumberFormatOptions`, for example `{ style: 'percent' }` or `{ style: 'currency', currency: 'GBP' }`. Store the numeric value, not the formatted display string.

`disabled` prevents focus, editing, and stepping. `readOnly` keeps a controlled value immutable. Set `invalid` and render `Error` for application validation. Empty input is supported through `null`. The application owns loading and asynchronous validation.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and internationalisation

- Tab focuses the spinbutton. Up Arrow increments and Down Arrow decrements by `step`.
- Page Up and Page Down perform larger steps where supported; Home and End move to finite bounds where supported.
- Typing, deletion, and locale-appropriate decimal and grouping characters use platform and locale behaviour.
- Always provide `Label`. Describe range, unit, or special formatting in `Description`; do not put essential unit information only in a visual addon.
- Use `formatOptions` for localisation rather than manually adding currency symbols to the stored value.

Formatting follows the `BreezeProvider` locale and the surrounding direction. Right-to-left layout reverses visual ordering appropriately while the number remains locale formatted.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not store the formatted text, use a number field for an identifier, pass numeric state to `Input`, or omit step-button names. Be careful with binary floating-point for money; convert to an appropriate minor-unit or decimal representation at the domain boundary. Use `Slider` for bounded direct manipulation, `TextField` for numeric-looking strings, and `InputGroup` for a purely visual unit when locale formatting is not appropriate.

Nonmodal Outside Dismissal story ok
nonmodal filter popover with outside dismissal
Opens a nonmodal filter popover, exercises its nested Select, verifies wide action layout, and checks outside plus explicit dismissal paths.
function NonmodalOutsideDismissal() {
  return (
    <ButtonGroup>
      <Popover.Root>
        <Popover.Trigger>Show filters</Popover.Trigger>
        <Popover.Content placement="bottom start">
          <Popover.Title>Filters</Popover.Title>
          <Popover.Description>
            Optional controls remain nonmodal.
          </Popover.Description>
          <Stack gap="xl">
            <Select.Root defaultValue="any">
              <Select.Label>Status</Select.Label>
              <Select.Trigger>
                <Select.Value />
              </Select.Trigger>
              <Select.Popover>
                <Select.ListBox>
                  <Select.Item id="any" textValue="Any">
                    Any
                  </Select.Item>
                  <Select.Item id="active" textValue="Active">
                    Active
                  </Select.Item>
                </Select.ListBox>
              </Select.Popover>
            </Select.Root>
            <ButtonGroup
              align="end"
              orientation={{ base: 'vertical', sm: 'horizontal' }}
            >
              <Popover.Close>Done</Popover.Close>
            </ButtonGroup>
          </Stack>
        </Popover.Content>
      </Popover.Root>
      <Button appearance="outline" data-popover-outside-target>
        Outside target
      </Button>
    </ButtonGroup>
  );
}
Nonmodal Outside Dismissal Compact story ok
compact nonmodal popover with full-width action
Repeats the nonmodal filter workflow at the canonical compact viewport and verifies the close action expands into a stacked layout.
const NonmodalOutsideDismissalCompact = () => <Root />;
Modal And Read Only story ok
read-only open modal popover anatomy
Keeps a modal popover immutably open to document contained outside interaction, complete accessible anatomy, and an explicit close request.
const ModalAndReadOnly = () => <Root open readOnly>(<>
      <Popover.Trigger>Persistent details</Popover.Trigger>
      <Popover.Content modal>
        <Popover.Title>Modal popover</Popover.Title>
        <Popover.Description>
          Outside interaction is contained.
        </Popover.Description>
        <Popover.Close>Request close</Popover.Close>
      </Popover.Content>
    </>)</Root>;
Placement Extremes story ok
long popover content with top-end placement
Places long wrapping content at a preferred top-end position so automatic collision handling and the viewport-bounded surface can be inspected.
const PlacementExtremes = () => <Root>(<>
      <Popover.Trigger>Long content</Popover.Trigger>
      <Popover.Content placement="top end">
        <Popover.Title>
          A long title that wraps within the constrained surface
        </Popover.Title>
        <Popover.Description>
          Positioning flips automatically when viewport space is constrained.
        </Popover.Description>
        <Popover.Close>Close</Popover.Close>
      </Popover.Content>
    </>)</Root>;
Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/Popover/Popover.mdx
A trigger-anchored contextual dialog with required title and description, modal or nonmodal interaction, collision-aware placement, and controlled or uncontrolled open state.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Popover.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="A trigger-anchored contextual dialog with required title and description, modal or nonmodal interaction, collision-aware placement, and controlled or uncontrolled open state."
/>

# Popover

`Popover` places lightweight interactive content next to the control that requested it.

## Import

```tsx
import { Popover } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for contextual filters, settings, or explanations containing interactive controls. Use `Tooltip` for concise non-interactive help, `Menu` for a list of actions, `Dialog` for a focused modal task, and inline content when it should remain continuously visible.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Popover } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Popover.Root>
  <Popover.Trigger>Show details</Popover.Trigger>
  <Popover.Content>
    <Popover.Title>Details</Popover.Title>
    <Popover.Description>
      Supporting information about this item.
    </Popover.Description>
    <Popover.Close>Done</Popover.Close>
  </Popover.Content>
</Popover.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.NonmodalOutsideDismissal} />

## Anatomy and interaction modes

| Part                  | Purpose                                            |
| --------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| `Popover.Root`        | Coordinates open state and trigger focus.          |
| `Popover.Trigger`     | Opens the popover from a semantic button.          |
| `Popover.Content`     | Positioned dialog surface and dismissal boundary.  |
| `Popover.Title`       | Required accessible name.                          |
| `Popover.Description` | Required accessible description.                   |
| `Popover.Close`       | Explicit close action that restores trigger focus. |

Content is nonmodal by default, so users may interact outside and outside activation dismisses it. Set `modal` when interaction must remain inside. `dismissible` defaults to `true`; `keyboardDismissDisabled` defaults to `false`. Always retain a clear close path if either dismissal route is removed.

## State and placement

Use uncontrolled `defaultOpen`, controlled `open` with `onOpenChange`, or immutable `open` with `readOnly`. `onOpenChange` receives the complete next Boolean.

Placement defaults to `'bottom start'` and supports `top`, `bottom`, `left`, `right`, and top/bottom start/end combinations. React Aria flips or shifts the surface when space is constrained. `offset` is the trigger gap in pixels and defaults to `8`. Start/end and collision behaviour follow provider direction.

Loading, disabled, invalid, empty, and error states belong to controls or feedback within Content. A disabled Trigger cannot open the popover. Do not show an empty surface.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and callback semantics

Content accepts relevant dialog HTML and ARIA attributes. Use `onAction` on Trigger and Close rather than native click handlers. Breeze owns portal, positioning, role, and inline style behaviour.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and mistakes

Trigger activation opens Content and moves focus appropriately. Tab follows modal or nonmodal focus rules, Escape closes unless disabled, and Close restores focus. Title and Description are required even when the trigger appears descriptive. Ensure the surface remains understandable when translated text wraps and test preferred placement in RTL.

Do not use a Popover as a tooltip, omit its accessible anatomy, place a long workflow inside it, or assume preferred placement is guaranteed. Related components: `Tooltip`, `Menu`, `Dialog`, `Select`, and `ComboBox`.
Close 10 prop types

Prop types (react-docgen-typescript) 11 prop types 
Component: src/primitives/RadioGroup/RadioGroup.tsx::Root
Props:
/**
 * Compound label, radio items, description, and error parts.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Initial selected option. Defaults to no selection.
 */
defaultSelection?: string

/**
 * Prevents focus and selection changes for every option. Defaults to `false`.
 */
disabled?: boolean = false

/**
 * Exposes invalid group state to assistive technology and error styling. Defaults to `false`.
 */
invalid?: boolean = false

/**
 * Native form field name shared by every radio option.
 */
name?: string

/**
 * Called with the next selected option value.
 */
onSelectionChange?: ((selection: string) => void) | ((selection: string) => void)

/**
 * Option layout and arrow-key navigation axis. Defaults to `vertical`.
 */
orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" = vertical

/**
 * Marks a controlled selection as intentionally immutable.
 */
readOnly?: boolean = false

/**
 * Ref to the rendered radio group.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLDivElement>

/**
 * Marks the radio group as requiring a selection. Defaults to `false`.
 */
required?: boolean = false

/**
 * Current selected option, or `null` when no option is selected.
 * Current immutable option, or `null` when no option is selected.
 */
selection?: string | null

Anatomy And Horizontal Keyboard story ok
horizontal radio anatomy with arrow-key selection
Composes the complete horizontal group anatomy and verifies full-width guidance rows, canonical indicators, and ArrowRight selection movement.
const AnatomyAndHorizontalKeyboard = () => (
  <RadioGroup.Root
    defaultSelection="email"
    name="contact"
    orientation="horizontal"
  >
    <RadioGroup.Label>Contact method</RadioGroup.Label>
    <RadioGroup.Description>Choose one channel.</RadioGroup.Description>
    <RadioGroup.Item value="email">
      <RadioGroup.Control>
        <RadioGroup.Indicator />
        <RadioGroup.ItemLabel>Email</RadioGroup.ItemLabel>
      </RadioGroup.Control>
    </RadioGroup.Item>
    <RadioGroup.Item value="sms">
      <RadioGroup.Control>
        <RadioGroup.Indicator />
        <RadioGroup.ItemLabel>SMS</RadioGroup.ItemLabel>
      </RadioGroup.Control>
    </RadioGroup.Item>
    <RadioGroup.Item value="post">
      <RadioGroup.Control>
        <RadioGroup.Indicator />
        <RadioGroup.ItemLabel>Post</RadioGroup.ItemLabel>
      </RadioGroup.Control>
    </RadioGroup.Item>
  </RadioGroup.Root>
);

Keeps the selected contact method in application state and reports the current controlled value through associated group guidance.
const Controlled = () => <ControlledExample />;
Vertical Read Only And States story ok
vertical read-only disabled and invalid group states
Compares vertically arranged read-only, disabled, and required invalid groups so their distinct selection and validation contracts remain clear.
const VerticalReadOnlyAndStates = () => (
  <Stack gap="xl">
    <RadioGroup.Root readOnly selection="email">
      <RadioGroup.Label>Read-only</RadioGroup.Label>
      <Option label="Email" value="email" />
      <Option label="SMS" value="sms" />
    </RadioGroup.Root>
    <RadioGroup.Root disabled defaultSelection="post">
      <RadioGroup.Label>Disabled</RadioGroup.Label>
      <Option label="Post" value="post" />
    </RadioGroup.Root>
    <RadioGroup.Root invalid required>
      <RadioGroup.Label>Required</RadioGroup.Label>
      <Option label="Standard" value="standard" />
      <RadioGroup.Error>Select a method.</RadioGroup.Error>
    </RadioGroup.Root>
  </Stack>
);

const DisabledOptionKeyboard = () => (
  <RadioGroup.Root defaultSelection="first" orientation="vertical">
    <RadioGroup.Label>Priority</RadioGroup.Label>
    <Option label="First" value="first" />
    <RadioGroup.Item disabled value="second">
      <RadioGroup.Control>
        <RadioGroup.Indicator />
        <RadioGroup.ItemLabel>Second</RadioGroup.ItemLabel>
      </RadioGroup.Control>
    </RadioGroup.Item>
    <Option label="Third" value="third" />

);

  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <RadioGroup.Root>
      <RadioGroup.Label>
        A long exclusive-choice label in a constrained column
      </RadioGroup.Label>
      <Option
        description="Supporting option guidance remains associated."
        label="An unusually detailed radio option that wraps naturally"
        value="long"
      />
      <Option label="Short" value="short" />
    </RadioGroup.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);

./src/primitives/RadioGroup/RadioGroup.mdx
Labelled exclusive selection with native radio form semantics, per-option guidance, orientation-aware keyboard navigation, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, required, and invalid states.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './RadioGroup.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Labelled exclusive selection with native radio form semantics, per-option guidance, orientation-aware keyboard navigation, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, required, and invalid states."
/>

# RadioGroup

`RadioGroup` presents a visible set of options from which one string value may be selected.

## Import

```tsx
import { RadioGroup } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it when choices are mutually exclusive, the set is short, and comparing all options helps the decision. Prefer `Select` when space is constrained or there are many options, `CheckboxGroup` when several values may be selected, and `SegmentedControl` for a small view or mode switch. Do not use radio buttons for an action that takes effect without representing a persisted choice.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { RadioGroup } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<RadioGroup.Root name="frequency" defaultSelection="weekly">
  <RadioGroup.Label>Report frequency</RadioGroup.Label>
  <RadioGroup.Item value="daily">
    <RadioGroup.Control>
      <RadioGroup.Indicator />
      <RadioGroup.ItemLabel>Daily</RadioGroup.ItemLabel>
    </RadioGroup.Control>
  </RadioGroup.Item>
  <RadioGroup.Item value="weekly">
    <RadioGroup.Control>
      <RadioGroup.Indicator />
      <RadioGroup.ItemLabel>Weekly</RadioGroup.ItemLabel>
    </RadioGroup.Control>
  </RadioGroup.Item>
</RadioGroup.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.AnatomyAndHorizontalKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                         | Purpose                                                                   |
| ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `RadioGroup.Root`            | Owns exclusive selection, shared form name, orientation, and group state. |
| `RadioGroup.Label`           | Persistent accessible name for the set.                                   |
| `RadioGroup.Item`            | One keyed option with its stable string `value`.                          |
| `RadioGroup.Control`         | Native label and interaction target for one option.                       |
| `RadioGroup.Indicator`       | Decorative selected-state mark.                                           |
| `RadioGroup.ItemLabel`       | Persistent visible name inside the control.                               |
| `RadioGroup.ItemDescription` | Guidance associated with one option.                                      |
| `RadioGroup.Description`     | Guidance associated with the whole set.                                   |
| `RadioGroup.Error`           | Group validation feedback while invalid.                                  |

Put `Control` and optional `ItemDescription` inside `Item`; put `Indicator` and `ItemLabel` inside `Control`. Keep group-level guidance outside items.

## Selection, callbacks, and states

Use `defaultSelection` for uncontrolled state, `selection` with `onSelectionChange` for controlled state, or `selection` with `readOnly` for an immutable choice. `selection` is `string | null`; `null` means no selection. `onSelectionChange` receives the newly selected string.

`disabled` on `Root` disables every option; `disabled` on `Item` disables one. `readOnly` prevents changes to a controlled selection. `required` means an option must be selected. Set `invalid` and render `Error` for a group validation failure. Loading and an empty option set are application-owned; avoid rendering an empty radio group.

`orientation` is `vertical` by default. Use `horizontal` only for short labels and a small set; it also sets the expected arrow-key axis.

## Keyboard and accessibility

- Tab enters the group at the selected option, or the first enabled option when none is selected.
- Arrow keys move focus and selection through enabled options according to orientation; disabled items are skipped.
- Space selects the focused option where platform behaviour requires it.
- Provide both a group `Label` and an `ItemLabel` for every option. Do not rely on colour or the indicator alone.
- Stable `value` strings should be domain identifiers, not translated visible labels.

The component follows surrounding `dir`; horizontal arrow behaviour and layout adapt for right-to-left content.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not give options duplicate values, put `value` on `Control`, omit the group label, or use horizontal layout for content that wraps ambiguously. Use `Select` for a compact list, `CheckboxGroup` for multiple selection, and `SegmentedControl` for a compact mode choice.

/**
 * Allows ranges containing unavailable dates.
 */
allowsNonContiguousRanges?: boolean = false

/**
 * Calendar navigation, heading, range grid, and guidance.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Excluded when controlled.
 * Initial uncontrolled range, or `null` when empty.
 */
defaultValue?: DateRangeValue | null

/**
 * Prevents focus and selection.
 */
disabled?: boolean = false

/**
 * Exposes invalid selection state.
 */
invalid?: boolean = false

/**

/**

 */

/**
 * Earliest selectable date.
 */

/**
 * Called with the next stable range or `null`.
 * Excluded because read-only calendars cannot change.
 */
onChange?: ((value: DateRangeValue | null) => void) | ((value: DateRangeValue | null) => void)

/**
 * Mutable controlled calendars cannot be read-only.
 * Prevents range changes.
 * Uncontrolled calendars cannot be read-only.
 */
readOnly?: boolean = false

 */

 * Current controlled range, or `null` when empty.
 * Current immutable range, or `null` when empty.
 * Excluded when uncontrolled.
 */

 */

Uncontrolled Range story ok
uncontrolled inclusive date-range selection
Starts from an uncontrolled inclusive range and verifies selecting new start and end dates reports stable ISO strings to the application callback.
const UncontrolledRange = ({ onChange }) => (
  <RangeCalendar.Root
    defaultValue={{ end: '2026-07-17', start: '2026-07-13' }}
    onChange={onChange}
  >
    <RangeCalendar.Header>
      <RangeCalendar.PreviousButton />
      <RangeCalendar.Heading />
      <RangeCalendar.NextButton />
    </RangeCalendar.Header>
    <RangeCalendar.Grid />
  </RangeCalendar.Root>
);
Controlled And Read Only story ok
controlled and read-only date ranges side by side
Compares an application-controlled calendar with an immutable read-only calendar that displays the same stable selected range.
const ControlledAndReadOnly = () => (
  <Inline align="stretch" gap="xl" wrap={false}>
    <Controlled />
    <RangeCalendar.Root
      readOnly
      value={{ end: '2026-07-17', start: '2026-07-13' }}
    >
      <Parts />
    </RangeCalendar.Root>
  </Inline>
);
Boundaries Unavailable Invalid story ok
bounded invalid range with an unavailable date
Presents minimum and maximum boundaries, one unavailable date, and explicit invalid state around an existing range selection.
const BoundariesUnavailableInvalid = () => (
  <RangeCalendar.Root
    defaultValue={{ end: '2026-07-17', start: '2026-07-13' }}
    invalid
    isDateUnavailable={(date) => date === '2026-07-15'}
    maxValue="2026-07-31"
    minValue="2026-07-01"
  >
    <Parts />
  </RangeCalendar.Root>
);
Multiple Months Non Contiguous story ok
two-month non-contiguous range selection
Displays two adjacent months and allows a selected range to span unavailable dates, documenting the explicit non-contiguous selection mode.
const MultipleMonthsNonContiguous = () => (
  <RangeCalendar.Root
    allowsNonContiguousRanges
    defaultValue={{ end: '2026-08-02', start: '2026-07-28' }}
    visibleMonths={2}
  >
    <Parts />
  </RangeCalendar.Root>
);
Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/RangeCalendar/RangeCalendar.mdx
Always-visible locale-aware calendar for selecting an inclusive start and end date as stable YYYY-MM-DD strings, with compound navigation, constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './RangeCalendar.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Always-visible locale-aware calendar for selecting an inclusive start and end date as stable YYYY-MM-DD strings, with compound navigation, constraints, and controlled, uncontrolled, or read-only state."
/>

# RangeCalendar

`RangeCalendar` presents one or more month grids for selecting an inclusive start and end date.

## Import

```tsx
import { RangeCalendar } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use `RangeCalendar` when comparing dates visually is central and the calendar should remain visible. Use `DateRangePicker` for a compact labelled form control, `Calendar` for one date, and two independent `DateField` instances only when the dates are not one coherent range.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { RangeCalendar } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<RangeCalendar.Root defaultValue={{ start: '2026-07-20', end: '2026-07-24' }}>
  <RangeCalendar.Header>
    <RangeCalendar.PreviousButton />
    <RangeCalendar.Heading />
    <RangeCalendar.NextButton />
  </RangeCalendar.Header>
  <RangeCalendar.Grid />
</RangeCalendar.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.UncontrolledRange} />

## Anatomy

| Part                           | Purpose                                                    |
| ------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `RangeCalendar.Root`           | Owns range selection, visible months and date constraints. |
| `RangeCalendar.Header`         | Lays out navigation and the visible-period heading.        |
| `RangeCalendar.PreviousButton` | Moves to the previous calendar page.                       |
| `RangeCalendar.Heading`        | Labels the current visible period.                         |
| `RangeCalendar.NextButton`     | Moves to the next calendar page.                           |
| `RangeCalendar.Grid`           | Renders weekdays, days and range-selection semantics.      |

## Range behaviour and state

A complete value is `{ start: 'YYYY-MM-DD', end: 'YYYY-MM-DD' }`; both endpoints are inclusive and `start` must not follow `end`. Empty state is `null`. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives a complete stable range after selection.

By default, an unavailable date blocks a range that crosses it. Set `allowsNonContiguousRanges` only when the domain explicitly permits a range containing unavailable days. `isDateUnavailable(date, anchorDate)` receives the candidate ISO date and the current range-selection anchor, or `null` before selection begins. `minValue` and `maxValue` are inclusive boundaries.

`disabled` prevents focus and selection. `invalid` exposes an unacceptable selection but does not supply prose; render application-owned error text nearby. Read-only preserves navigation and inspection while preventing changes. Loading and persistence are application-owned.

## Keyboard, accessibility and internationalisation

- Arrow keys move focus by day or week; Home and End move within a week; Page Up and Page Down change the visible month. Enter or Space sets the range start and then its inclusive end.
- Keep `Heading` and navigation controls in the root so the grid has a clear visible period and operable paging.
- Week layout, weekday names, announcements and date formatting follow the provider locale. Directional navigation and icons follow provider direction.
- Range meaning must not rely only on background colour. Breeze exposes selected range states to assistive technology.
- Use `weekdayStyle="short"` by default; `long` needs more width and `narrow` can be ambiguous.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not use two unrelated scalar strings, exclusive-end semantics, date objects or locale-formatted values. Do not enable non-contiguous ranges merely to bypass an unavailable-date rule. Use `DateRangePicker` in forms, `Calendar` for one date, and `DateTimePicker` when each selection represents an instant rather than a calendar day.

      <SearchField.Root readOnly value="locked">
        <SearchField.Label>Read-only</SearchField.Label>
        <SearchControl />
      </SearchField.Root>
      <SearchField.Root disabled defaultValue="disabled">
        <SearchField.Label>Disabled</SearchField.Label>
        <SearchControl />
      </SearchField.Root>
      <SearchField.Root invalid>
        <SearchField.Label>Invalid search</SearchField.Label>
        <SearchControl />
        <SearchField.Error>Revise the query.</SearchField.Error>
      </SearchField.Root>

);
