./src/primitives/SearchField/SearchField.mdx
Labelled search query entry with explicit submission, conditional clearing, controlled and uncontrolled values, and accessible field states.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './SearchField.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Labelled search query entry with explicit submission, conditional clearing, controlled and uncontrolled values, and accessible field states."
/>

# SearchField

`SearchField` is a compound query input with search semantics, submission, and an optional clear action.

## Import

```tsx
import { SearchField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it when entered text filters or searches content and users benefit from clearing the whole query. Prefer `TextField` for ordinary data entry and `ComboBox` when the input suggests or selects options from a collection. Do not use `SearchField` as a generic input merely to obtain its clear button.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { SearchField } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<SearchField.Root onSubmit={(query) => runSearch(query)}>
  <SearchField.Label>Search records</SearchField.Label>
  <SearchField.Group>
    <SearchField.Input name="query" placeholder="Name or reference" />
    <SearchField.ClearButton />
  </SearchField.Group>
  <SearchField.Description>Enter one or more keywords.</SearchField.Description>
</SearchField.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.AnatomyAndKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                      | Purpose                                                                             |
| ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `SearchField.Root`        | Owns query value, search submission, clearing, and field state.                     |
| `SearchField.Label`       | Persistently names the query input.                                                 |
| `SearchField.Group`       | Presents the input and clear action as one control and synchronises their size.     |
| `SearchField.Input`       | Renders a native `type="search"` input.                                             |
| `SearchField.ClearButton` | Clears a non-empty mutable query; it is omitted when empty, disabled, or read-only. |
| `SearchField.Description` | Associates query guidance.                                                          |
| `SearchField.Error`       | Associates validation feedback while invalid.                                       |

`Input` can be used without `Group`, but the canonical composition places `Input` and `ClearButton` inside `Group`.

## Value, callbacks, and states

Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives the next query string. `onSubmit` receives the current query when the user submits. `onClear` runs after the value is cleared by the clear button or Escape.

`disabled` removes interaction. `readOnly` keeps the query immutable and hides the clear action. `invalid`, `required`, `Description`, and `Error` follow the `TextField` contract. An empty query is `''`; `ClearButton` does not render for it. The application owns loading, results, empty-result messages, errors, and request cancellation.

## Keyboard and accessibility

- Tab focuses the search input and then any rendered clear button.
- Enter invokes `onSubmit` with the current query.
- Escape clears a non-empty mutable query and invokes `onClear`; native browser behaviour may require a second Escape to move focus or close an ancestor overlay.
- The clear button uses the provider’s localised `clear` message by default. Supply `aria-label` when product wording needs to be more specific.
- Provide a persistent `Label`. Announce result counts and asynchronous states in the result region, not by repeatedly changing the field label.
- Search results should have a clear heading or live-region strategy and sensible focus behaviour; `SearchField` does not render results.

`Group` supports `sm`, `md` (default), and `lg`, applying the size to its input and clear action. It follows surrounding writing direction and uses logical layout. Search strings are ordinary Unicode strings.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not manage clearing with a second button outside `Root`, assume `onSubmit` starts a request automatically, or display a clear button for read-only content. Use `TextField` for ordinary text, `ComboBox` for suggestions and option selection, and `ListBox` for a persistent result collection.

Prop types (react-docgen-typescript) 12 prop types 
Component: src/primitives/Select/Select.tsx::Root
Props:
/**
 * Compound label, trigger, popover, listbox, description, and error parts.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Excluded when value is controlled.
 * Initial selected key.
 */
defaultValue?: CollectionKey

/**
 * Prevents focus and selection. Defaults to `false`.
 */
disabled?: boolean = false

/**
 * Associates the native form value with an external form.
 */
form?: string

/**
 * Exposes invalid state. Defaults to `false`.
 */
invalid?: boolean = false

/**
 * Native form field name.
 */
name?: string

/**
 * Called when the selected key changes.
 * Excluded because read-only values cannot change.
 */
onChange?: ((value: CollectionKey | null) => void) | ((value: CollectionKey | null) => void)

/**
 * Placeholder shown before selection.
 */
placeholder?: string

/**
 * Controlled mutable state cannot be marked read-only.
 * Prevents interaction with the controlled value.
 * Uncontrolled state cannot be marked read-only.
 */
readOnly?: boolean = false

/**
 * Ref to the rendered field root.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLDivElement>

/**
 * Marks the select as required. Defaults to `false`.
 */
required?: boolean = false

/**
 * Current selected key, or `null` when no option is selected.
 * Current immutable selected key, or `null` when no option is selected.
 * Excluded when value is uncontrolled.
 */
value?: CollectionKey | null

Static Keyboard story ok
static select options with keyboard selection

const StaticKeyboard = () => (
  <Select.Root>
    <Select.Label>Country</Select.Label>
    <Select.Trigger>
      <Select.Value />
    </Select.Trigger>
    <Select.Popover>
      <Select.ListBox>
        <Select.Item id="gb" textValue="United Kingdom">
          United Kingdom
        </Select.Item>
        <Select.Item id="fr" textValue="France">
          France
        </Select.Item>
      </Select.ListBox>
    </Select.Popover>
  </Select.Root>
);

  <Select.Root>
    <Parts />

);

controlled and read-only select values

    <Select.Root readOnly value="fr">
      <Parts />

disabled and invalid select form states

  <form id="form">
    <Select.Root disabled name="country">
      <Parts />
    </Select.Root>
    <Select.Root invalid required name="required">
      <Parts />
    </Select.Root>

);

open select with application-owned empty content

const EmptyCollection = () => (
  <Select.Root placeholder="No countries available">
    <Select.Label>Country</Select.Label>
    <Select.Trigger>
      <Select.Value />
    </Select.Trigger>
    <Select.Popover>
      <Select.ListBox emptyContent="No countries">{null}</Select.ListBox>
    </Select.Popover>
  </Select.Root>
);

  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <Select.Root placeholder="Choose an option with a long placeholder">
      <Parts />
    </Select.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);
Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/Select/Select.mdx
Compact single selection from static or typed keyed options with a trigger, popover listbox, controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, validation, empty, and native form states.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Select.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Compact single selection from static or typed keyed options with a trigger, popover listbox, controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, validation, empty, and native form states."
/>

# Select

`Select` provides compact single selection from a known collection of keyed options.

## Import

```tsx
import { Select } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it when users must choose one known value and keeping every option visible would consume too much space. Prefer `RadioGroup` for a short comparison-sensitive set, `ComboBox` when users need filtering or free-form entry, and `ListBox` for persistent or multiple selection. Do not use Select for navigation; use links, menus, or a navigation component.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Select } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Select.Root name="region" placeholder="Choose a region">
  <Select.Label>Region</Select.Label>
  <Select.Trigger>
    <Select.Value />
  </Select.Trigger>
  <Select.Popover>
    <Select.ListBox>
      <Select.Item id="north" textValue="North">
        North
      </Select.Item>
      <Select.Item id="south" textValue="South">
        South
      </Select.Item>
    </Select.ListBox>
  </Select.Popover>
</Select.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.StaticKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                 | Purpose                                                            |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `Select.Root`        | Owns selected key, validation, placeholder, and native form value. |
| `Select.Label`       | Persistently names the select.                                     |
| `Select.Trigger`     | Opens and closes the popup and contains the displayed value.       |
| `Select.Value`       | Displays the selected option text or root placeholder.             |
| `Select.Popover`     | Positions option content relative to the trigger.                  |
| `Select.ListBox`     | Renders static or typed generic options and the empty state.       |
| `Select.Item`        | One option with a stable string or number key and `textValue`.     |
| `Select.Description` | Supporting guidance associated with the select.                    |
| `Select.Error`       | Validation feedback while invalid.                                 |

Keep `Value` inside `Trigger`, and `ListBox` inside `Popover`. A `ListBox` item’s `id` is its public value; visible content can be localised independently.

## Collections and selection

Static collections contain authored `Select.Item` children. Dynamic collections pass `items`, where each item has a stable `id: string | number`, and a child renderer.

```tsx
<Select.ListBox items={regions}>
  {(region) => (
    <Select.Item id={region.id} textValue={region.name}>
      {region.name}
    </Select.Item>
  )}
</Select.ListBox>
```

Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled selection, `value` with `onChange` for controlled selection, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable selection. The value is `string | number | null`; `null` means no option. `onChange` receives the next key or `null`.

`disabled` prevents focus and selection. `readOnly` is implemented as non-interactive while retaining the controlled value. `required` and `invalid` expose validation semantics. `emptyContent` defaults to `'No options'`. Loading options is application-owned; do not replace the collection without preserving stable keys and a clear status.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and internationalisation

- Tab focuses the trigger. Enter, Space, or Arrow Down opens the listbox; Escape closes it.
- Arrow keys move the active option, typeahead matches each item’s `textValue`, and Enter or Space selects according to platform behaviour.
- The popup returns focus to the trigger when dismissed.
- Always provide `Label`; provide accurate `textValue` when option content is not plain text.
- `Trigger` supports `sm`, `md` (default), and `lg`.
- Layout follows surrounding `dir`; keys remain stable while labels and empty content may be translated.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not use array indexes as keys, omit `textValue` for visual option content, place links or buttons inside options, or use a Select to navigate. Use `ComboBox` for filtering, `RadioGroup` for visible comparison, and `ListBox` for persistent or multiple selection.

Component: src/primitives/Slider/Slider.tsx::Root
Props:
/**
 * Compound label, output, track, fill, thumb, description, and error parts.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Initial slider value. Defaults to the minimum value.
 */
defaultValue?: number

/**
 * Prevents focus, dragging, and value changes. Defaults to `false`.
 */
disabled?: boolean = false

/**
 * Locale-aware number formatting options.
 */
formatOptions?: NumberFormatOptions

/**
 * Exposes invalid state to the thumb input and error styling. Defaults to `false`.
 */
invalid?: boolean = false

/**
 * Largest permitted value. Defaults to `100`.
 */
max?: number

/**
 * Smallest permitted value. Defaults to `0`.
 */
min?: number

/**
 * Called with the next slider value.
 */
onChange?: ((value: number) => void) | ((value: number) => void)

/**
 * Called with the final value after a drag or keyboard sequence ends.
 */
onChangeEnd?: ((value: number) => void)

/**
 * Track and keyboard direction. Defaults to `horizontal`.
 */
orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" = horizontal

/**
 * Marks a controlled value as intentionally immutable and non-interactive.
 */
readOnly?: boolean = false

/**
 * Ref to the rendered slider group.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLDivElement>

/**
 * Marks the native range input as required. Defaults to `false`.
 */
required?: boolean = false

/**
 * Keyboard and drag increment. Defaults to `1`.
 */
step?: number

/**
 * Current slider value.
 * Current immutable slider value.
 */
value?: number
Imports 
import { Slider } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Horizontal story ok
uncontrolled horizontal slider with keyboard stepping
Demonstrates an uncontrolled horizontal range with a visible label, locale-formatted output, native form name, and associated guidance.
const Horizontal = () => (
  <Slider.Root defaultValue={20} max={100} min={0} step={5}>
    <Slider.Label>Volume</Slider.Label>
    <Slider.Output />
    <Slider.Track>
      <Slider.Fill />
      <Slider.Thumb name="volume" />
    </Slider.Track>
    <Slider.Description>Use arrow keys or drag the thumb.</Slider.Description>
  </Slider.Root>
);
Controlled story ok
application-controlled single slider value
Keeps the numeric value in application state and reflects every semantic change in supporting text beneath the slider.
const Controlled = () => <ControlledExample />;
Vertical story ok
vertical slider geometry and keyboard boundaries
Uses vertical geometry and verifies orientation-aware Arrow, Home, and End key behaviour while keeping the label and output aligned.
const Vertical = () => (
  <Slider.Root defaultValue={5} max={10} min={0} orientation="vertical">
    <Slider.Label>Level</Slider.Label>
    <Slider.Output />
    <Track />
    <Slider.Description>Choose a level.</Slider.Description>
  </Slider.Root>
);
Read Only And States story ok
read-only disabled and invalid slider states
Compares immutable, disabled, and required invalid sliders while exercising the supported thumb sizes and validation-message association.
const ReadOnlyAndStates = () => (
  <Stack gap="xxl">
    <Slider.Root readOnly value={40}>
      <Slider.Label>Read-only</Slider.Label>
      <Slider.Output />
      <Track />
    </Slider.Root>
    <Slider.Root disabled defaultValue={50}>
      <Slider.Label>Disabled</Slider.Label>
      <Slider.Output />
      <Track size="sm" />
    </Slider.Root>
    <Slider.Root invalid required defaultValue={80}>
      <Slider.Label>Invalid</Slider.Label>
      <Slider.Output />
      <Track size="lg" />
      <Slider.Error>Choose an allowed value.</Slider.Error>
    </Slider.Root>
  </Stack>
);
Formatting And Extremes story ok
large decimal range and narrow content extreme
Constrains a large signed decimal range inside a narrow host to demonstrate locale formatting, precision, long copy, and native form participation.
const FormattingAndExtremes = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <Slider.Root
      defaultValue={999999.99}
      formatOptions={{ maximumFractionDigits: 2 }}
      max={1000000}
      min={-1000000}
      step={0.01}
    >
      <Slider.Label>A long bounded decimal range label</Slider.Label>
      <Slider.Output />
      <Track name="amount" />
      <Slider.Description>
        Large values remain locale-formatted while the native range input
        participates in forms.
      </Slider.Description>
    </Slider.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);

./src/primitives/Slider/Slider.mdx
Accessible single-value range input with explicit label, formatted output, track, fill, thumb, guidance, validation, horizontal or vertical orientation, and controlled, uncontrolled, and read-only state.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Slider.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Accessible single-value range input with explicit label, formatted output, track, fill, thumb, guidance, validation, horizontal or vertical orientation, and controlled, uncontrolled, and read-only state."
/>

# Slider

`Slider` selects one numeric value in a bounded range through pointer, touch, or keyboard interaction.

## Import

```tsx
import { Slider } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it when relative position and quick adjustment matter more than typing an exact value, such as volume or intensity. Prefer `NumberField` for exact numeric entry, unbounded values, or values where comparing digits is essential. Do not use a slider when the range or current value cannot be explained clearly.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Slider } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Slider.Root defaultValue={40} min={0} max={100} step={5}>
  <Slider.Label>Volume</Slider.Label>
  <Slider.Output />
  <Slider.Track>
    <Slider.Fill />
    <Slider.Thumb name="volume" />
  </Slider.Track>
  <Slider.Description>Adjust from 0 to 100.</Slider.Description>
</Slider.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Horizontal} />

## Anatomy

| Part                 | Purpose                                                            |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `Slider.Root`        | Owns value, range, step, orientation, formatting, and field state. |
| `Slider.Label`       | Persistently names the slider.                                     |
| `Slider.Output`      | Displays the locale-formatted current value or custom content.     |
| `Slider.Track`       | Pointer and touch interaction surface.                             |
| `Slider.Fill`        | Decorative selected portion of the track.                          |
| `Slider.Thumb`       | Draggable focus target and hidden native range input.              |
| `Slider.Description` | Guidance associated with the thumb.                                |
| `Slider.Error`       | Validation feedback associated with the thumb while invalid.       |

Keep `Fill` and `Thumb` inside `Track`; keep all parts inside `Root`. This API is single-value only: render another component if the domain needs a range with two thumbs.

## Value and callbacks

Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives each next number during interaction. `onChangeEnd` receives the final number after a drag or keyboard sequence, which is useful for an expensive commit while `onChange` keeps the interface responsive.

`min` defaults to `0`, `max` to `100`, and `step` to `1`. `formatOptions` accepts `Intl.NumberFormatOptions`; `Output` uses it and may be replaced with custom children when the same value meaning remains accessible. `disabled` prevents focus and changes. `readOnly` disables the thumb while preserving the controlled value. `invalid` and `required` are applied to the hidden range input; render `Error` for recovery guidance. Loading is application-owned.

## Orientation, size, and formatting

`orientation` defaults to `horizontal`; vertical layout provides a 256-pixel track. `Thumb` sizes are `sm`, `md` (default), and `lg`. Choose a step that divides the meaningful domain and avoid excessive precision. Use locale formatting for percentages, units, or currency rather than manually concatenating symbols.

## Keyboard and accessibility

- Tab focuses the thumb. Arrow keys change by `step`; Page Up and Page Down make larger changes; Home and End move to minimum and maximum.
- Pointer or touch on the track moves the value; dragging the thumb updates continuously.
- Provide `Label`. `aria-label` on `Thumb` is available when the root label cannot sufficiently name the thumb, but is usually unnecessary for a single-value slider.
- Communicate the range and unit in `Description` when they are not obvious. Do not rely only on position or colour.
- Layout, track direction, keyboard interpretation, and number formatting follow locale and surrounding `dir`.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not use Slider for exact entry, omit a visible or accessible value, choose meaningless fractional steps, or perform expensive persistence on every `onChange` when `onChangeEnd` suffices. Use `NumberField` for exact numbers, `ProgressBar` for read-only progress, and `Meter` for a read-only measurement.

        indicator={<CheckIcon />}
        status="complete"

      <Stepper.Item
        description="Registered identity and contact details."
        indicator={<CheckIcon />}
        status="complete"
        title="Details"
      />
      <Stepper.Item
        description="Preferences and opening values."
        indicator="2"
        status="current"
        title="Settings"
      />
    </>)</Root>;
Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/Stepper/Stepper.mdx
Accessible ordered progression showing complete, current, and upcoming application-owned stages.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Stepper.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Accessible ordered progression showing complete, current, and upcoming application-owned stages."
/>

# Stepper

`Stepper` describes named stages in a process as an ordered list with one current-step marker.

```tsx
import { Stepper } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
import { CheckIcon } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui/icons';
```

## When to use

Use a stepper when people benefit from knowing their position in a small, stable multi-stage process.

## When not to use

Use [ProgressBar](?path=/docs/feedback-progressbar--docs) for continuous task completion, [Tabs](?path=/docs/primitives-navigation-tabs--docs) for switching peer views, and [Breadcrumbs](?path=/docs/primitives-navigation-breadcrumbs--docs) for location in a hierarchy.

Stepper is descriptive, not navigation: it does not change pages, validate data, or persist completion.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { Stepper } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
import { CheckIcon } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui/icons';

export function SetupProgress() {
  return (
    <Stepper.Root aria-label="Account setup">
      <Stepper.Item
        indicator={<CheckIcon />}
        status="complete"
        title="Details"
      />
      <Stepper.Item indicator="2" status="current" title="Settings" />
      <Stepper.Item indicator="3" status="upcoming" title="Review" />
    </Stepper.Root>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.Horizontal} />

## Anatomy

- `Stepper.Root` renders the ordered-list container and controls visual orientation.
- `Stepper.Item` renders one list item, its decorative indicator, visible title, and optional description.

## Status and orientation

Every item requires `status`: `complete`, `current`, or `upcoming`. `current` adds `aria-current="step"`. Keep exactly one current item during an active flow; the indicator is intentionally hidden from assistive technology, so the visible translated `title` and status semantics must carry meaning. `orientation` is `horizontal` by default and may be `vertical`; it changes layout, not reading order or keyboard behaviour.

<Canvas of={Stories.VerticalWithDescriptions} />

## Accessibility, state, and direction

Give the root an accessible name with `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` when surrounding context does not name it. Use meaningful text titles, not icon-only titles. Stepper has no focus management, keyboard interaction, callbacks, controlled/uncontrolled value, loading, disabled, invalid, empty, or error state; the application derives statuses from its own state. Translate titles, descriptions, and the root name. Source order remains progression order in both directions.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not make list items clickable without real links or buttons.
- Do not mark multiple items current.
- Do not communicate completion only with indicator colour or an icon.
- Do not reorder items visually without changing their semantic source order.

## Related components

[ProgressBar](?path=/docs/feedback-progressbar--docs), [Tabs](?path=/docs/primitives-navigation-tabs--docs), and [Breadcrumbs](?path=/docs/primitives-navigation-breadcrumbs--docs).
Item 5 prop types
/home/runner/work/platform/platform/packages/breeze-ui/src/primitives/Stepper/Stepper.tsx

 */
required?: boolean = false

 */

Imports 
import { Switch } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Anatomy And Keyboard story ok
uncontrolled labelled switch with keyboard activation
Exercises the complete uncontrolled switch anatomy, Space-key toggling, native form value, and description association for one immediate setting.
const AnatomyAndKeyboard = () => (
  <Switch.Root name="alerts" value="email">
    <Switch.Control>
      <Switch.Track>
        <Switch.Thumb />
      </Switch.Track>
      <Switch.Label>Email alerts</Switch.Label>
    </Switch.Control>
    <Switch.Description>
      Receive important workspace activity.
    </Switch.Description>
  </Switch.Root>
);
Controlled story ok
application-controlled Boolean setting
Keeps the selected Boolean in application state and reflects each semantic change through concise supporting status text.
const Controlled = () => <ControlledExample />;
Read Only And States story ok
read-only disabled and invalid switch states
Compares immutable selected, disabled selected, and required invalid switch states while demonstrating the default and large control geometries.
const ReadOnlyAndStates = () => (
  <Stack gap="compact">
    <Switch.Root readOnly selected>
      <Control label="Read-only enabled" />
    </Switch.Root>
    <Switch.Root disabled defaultSelected>
      <Control label="Disabled" />
    </Switch.Root>
    <Switch.Root invalid required>
      <Control label="Required setting" size="lg" />
      <Switch.Error>Enable this setting.</Switch.Error>
    </Switch.Root>
  </Stack>
);
Native Form story ok
selected switch with native form participation
Associates the hidden native checkbox with an external form id and submits the configured name and value only while selected.
const NativeForm = () => (
  <form id="preferences" onSubmit={(event) => event.preventDefault()}>
    <Switch.Root
      defaultSelected
      form="preferences"
      name="security"
      value="enabled"
    >
      <Control label="Security notifications" />
    </Switch.Root>
  </form>
);
Content Extreme story ok
long switch content in a narrow layout
Constrains long label and description copy to a narrow host while preserving the full touch target and accessible text association.
const ContentExtreme = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <Switch.Root>
      <Control label="A long switch label that wraps without reducing the touch target" />
      <Switch.Description>
        Detailed guidance remains associated in narrow layouts.
      </Switch.Description>
    </Switch.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);
Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/Switch/Switch.mdx
Accessible compound on/off setting with visible label, native checkbox form participation, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, required, and invalid states.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Switch.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Accessible compound on/off setting with visible label, native checkbox form participation, and controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, required, and invalid states."
/>

# Switch

`Switch` represents a Boolean setting whose on or off state usually takes effect immediately.

## Import

```tsx
import { Switch } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for persistent settings such as enabling notifications or a display preference. Prefer `Checkbox` for selection, acknowledgement, or a value submitted as part of a larger form. Do not use a switch to trigger a one-off action; use `Button`.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Switch } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Switch.Root defaultSelected name="updates">
  <Switch.Control>
    <Switch.Track>
      <Switch.Thumb />
    </Switch.Track>
    <Switch.Label>Email updates</Switch.Label>
  </Switch.Control>
  <Switch.Description>Changes take effect immediately.</Switch.Description>
</Switch.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.AnatomyAndKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                 | Purpose                                                                      |
| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Switch.Root`        | Owns Boolean state, validation, and hidden native checkbox form integration. |
| `Switch.Control`     | Native label and complete pointer, touch, and keyboard target.               |
| `Switch.Track`       | Decorative on/off track.                                                     |
| `Switch.Thumb`       | Decorative moving thumb inside the track.                                    |
| `Switch.Label`       | Persistent visible name inside the control.                                  |
| `Switch.Description` | Supporting guidance associated with the switch.                              |
| `Switch.Error`       | Validation feedback associated while invalid.                                |

Keep `Thumb` inside `Track`, and `Track` plus `Label` inside `Control`. Track and thumb are hidden from assistive technology; native switch state carries the meaning.

## State, callbacks, and forms

Use `defaultSelected` for uncontrolled state, `selected` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `selected` with `readOnly` for immutable state. `onChange` receives the complete next Boolean.

When on, the hidden native checkbox submits `name=value`; `value` defaults to `'on'`. It submits nothing when off. `form` associates it with an external form. `disabled` prevents focus and changes and removes normal submission. `readOnly` preserves controlled state without changes. `required` and `invalid` are available, though switches rarely make sense as required acknowledgements—prefer `Checkbox` for consent.

Loading is application-owned. If a change is saved asynchronously, keep the switch’s state truthful, communicate progress separately, and handle failure without silently reverting.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and direction

- Tab focuses the switch; Space toggles it. Clicking the visible label also toggles it.
- The `Label` should describe the setting, not instruct “turn on”. State is announced separately.
- Do not add “on/off” text that conflicts with the native announced state.
- `Track` and `Thumb` each support `sm`, `md` (default), and `lg`; use the same size for both while retaining the control’s large hit target.
- Logical layout and thumb movement follow surrounding direction.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not use Switch for submit-time choices, mismatch track and thumb sizes, put interactive content inside its label, or show stale optimistic state after a failed save. Use `Checkbox` for acknowledgement, `ToggleButton` for a pressed command, and `SegmentedControl` for mutually exclusive modes.

collections-table · ./src/primitives/Table/Table.stories.tsx
Coordinates semantic table navigation, row state, sorting, and responsive labels.
Prop types (react-docgen-typescript) 14 prop types 
Component: src/primitives/Table/Table.tsx::Root
Props:
/**
 * Optional visual treatment for the table's lower edge. Defaults to `none`.
 */
boundary?: TableBoundary = none

/**
 * Ordered header, body, and optional footer sections.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Excluded when selection is controlled.
 * Initial selected row keys. Defaults to an empty selection.
 */
defaultSelection?: CollectionSelection

/**
 * Excluded when sorting is controlled.
 * Initial sort descriptor.
 */
defaultSort?: TableSort

/**
 * Keys whose rows cannot receive focus, selection, or actions.
 */
disabledKeys?: Iterable<CollectionKey>

/**
 * Responsive native table, responsive desktop grid, or persistent CSS grid rows. Defaults to `responsive`.
 */
layout?: TableLayout = responsive

/**
 * Enables multiple row selection. Defaults to `false`.
 */
multiple?: boolean = false

/**
 * Called with the next selected row keys.
 * Excluded because read-only selection cannot change.
 */
onSelectionChange?: ((selection: CollectionSelection) => void) | ((selection: CollectionSelection) => void)

/**
 * Called when a sortable heading requests a new descriptor.
 * Called when the internally retained descriptor changes.
 */
onSortChange?: ((sort: TableSort) => void) | ((sort: TableSort) => void)

/**
 * Controlled mutable state cannot be marked read-only.
 * Marks controlled row selection and sorting as intentionally immutable.
 * Uncontrolled state cannot be marked read-only.
 */
readOnly?: boolean = false

/**
 * Ref to the rendered table or virtualized grid element.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLElement>

/**
 * Current selected row keys.
 * Current immutable selected row keys.
 * Excluded when selection is uncontrolled.
 */
selection?: CollectionSelection

/**
 * Current consumer-owned sort descriptor.
 * Excluded when sorting is uncontrolled.
 */
sort?: TableSort

/**
 * Optional fixed- or variable-height row windowing configuration.
 */
virtualization?: CollectionVirtualization
Imports 
import { Table } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Controlled Sorting And Selection story ok
explicitly authored table with controlled sorting and selection
Authors columns and rows directly while the application controls selected keys and the consumer-owned sort descriptor.
function ControlledSortingAndSelection() {
  const [selection, setSelection] = useState<CollectionSelection>([1]);
  const [sort, setSort] = useState<TableSort>({
    column: 'name',
    direction: 'ascending',
  });

  return (
    <Table.Root
      aria-label="Controlled items"
      onSelectionChange={setSelection}
      onSortChange={setSort}
      selection={selection}
      sort={sort}
    >
      <Table.Header id="headings">
        <Table.Column id="name" rowHeader sortable>
          Name
        </Table.Column>
        <Table.Column id="state" sortable>
          State
        </Table.Column>
      </Table.Header>
      <Table.Body id="items">
        <Table.Row id={1} textValue="Alpha Ready">
          <Table.Cell column="name">Alpha</Table.Cell>
          <Table.Cell column="state">Ready</Table.Cell>
        </Table.Row>
        <Table.Row id={2} textValue="Beta In review">
          <Table.Cell column="name">Beta</Table.Cell>
          <Table.Cell column="state">In review</Table.Cell>
        </Table.Row>
        <Table.Row id={3} textValue="Gamma Draft">
          <Table.Cell column="name">Gamma</Table.Cell>
          <Table.Cell column="state">Draft</Table.Cell>
        </Table.Row>
      </Table.Body>
    </Table.Root>
  );
}
Static Ordered Sections story ok
static ordered header body and footer sections
Composes keyed static header, body, and footer sections whose cells follow the visible heading order and retain long-value wrapping.
const StaticOrderedSections = () => (
  <Table.Root aria-label="Static data">
    <Table.Header id="heading-section">
      <Table.Column id="label" rowHeader>
        Label
      </Table.Column>
      <Table.Column id="value">Value</Table.Column>
    </Table.Header>
    <Table.Body id="body-section">
      <Table.Row id="first" textValue="First Long value">
        <Table.Cell column="label">First</Table.Cell>
        <Table.Cell column="value">
          A long value that wraps in the responsive card presentation
        </Table.Cell>
      </Table.Row>
    </Table.Body>
    <Table.Footer id="footer-section">
      <Table.Row id="summary" textValue="Summary One item">
        <Table.Cell column="label">Summary</Table.Cell>
        <Table.Cell column="value">One item</Table.Cell>
      </Table.Row>
    </Table.Footer>
  </Table.Root>
);

table with a strong lower boundary
Adds the canonical strong lower boundary without introducing top or side borders, preserving the table's relationship to surrounding content.

  <Table.Root aria-label={ariaLabel} boundary={boundary}>
    <Table.Header>
      <Table.Column id="name" rowHeader>
        Name
      </Table.Column>
      <Table.Column id="state">State</Table.Column>
    </Table.Header>
    <Table.Body>
      <Table.Row id="aurora" textValue="Aurora Ready">
        <Table.Cell column="name">Aurora</Table.Cell>
        <Table.Cell column="state">Ready</Table.Cell>
      </Table.Row>
    </Table.Body>
  </Table.Root>
);

    aria-label="Grouped grid items"
    className="grid-cols-[minmax(0,1fr)_auto]"
    layout="grid"
  >
    <Table.Header>

        Name
      </Table.Column>

    </Table.Header>

      <Table.Row id="alpha" textValue="Alpha Ready">
        <Table.Cell column="name">Alpha</Table.Cell>
        <Table.Cell column="state">Ready</Table.Cell>
      </Table.Row>
      <Table.Row id="beta" textValue="Beta Review">
        <Table.Cell column="name">Beta</Table.Cell>
        <Table.Cell column="state">Review</Table.Cell>
      </Table.Row>
    </Table.Body>
    <Table.Body id="archived">
      <Table.Row id="gamma" textValue="Gamma Draft">
        <Table.Cell column="name">Gamma</Table.Cell>
        <Table.Cell column="state">Draft</Table.Cell>
      </Table.Row>
    </Table.Body>
  </Table.Root>
);

        <Table.Cell column="state">Draft</Table.Cell>
      </Table.Row>

);

const ReadOnlyAndEmpty = () => (
  <Stack gap="xl">
    <Table.Root aria-label="Read-only" readOnly selection={[1]}>
      <Table.Header>
        <Table.Column id="name" rowHeader>
          Name
        </Table.Column>
      </Table.Header>
      <Table.Body>
        <Table.Row id={1} textValue="Alpha">
          <Table.Cell column="name">Alpha</Table.Cell>
        </Table.Row>
      </Table.Body>
    </Table.Root>
    <Table.Root aria-label="Empty">
      <Table.Header>
        <Table.Column id="name" rowHeader>
          Name
        </Table.Column>
      </Table.Header>
      <Table.Body emptyContent="No items">{null}</Table.Body>
    </Table.Root>
  </Stack>
);
Variable Virtualization And Loading story ok
variable-height virtualized rows with loading sentinel
Windows variable-height rows inside a bounded viewport and renders a loading sentinel while preserving desktop heading and cell geometry.
function VariableVirtualizationAndLoading() {
  return (
    <Table.Root
      aria-label="Virtual data"
      virtualization={{
        estimatedRowHeight: 52,
        mode: 'variable',
        overscan: 80,
        viewportHeight: 156,
      }}
    >
      <Table.Header>
        <Table.Column id="name" rowHeader>
          Name
        </Table.Column>
        <Table.Column id="state">State</Table.Column>
      </Table.Header>
      <Table.Body>
        <Table.Row id={1} textValue="Alpha Ready">
          <Table.Cell column="name">Alpha</Table.Cell>
          <Table.Cell column="state">Ready</Table.Cell>
        </Table.Row>
        <Table.Row id={2} textValue="Beta In review">
          <Table.Cell column="name">Beta</Table.Cell>
          <Table.Cell column="state">In review</Table.Cell>
        </Table.Row>
        <Table.Row id={3} textValue="Gamma Draft">
          <Table.Cell column="name">Gamma</Table.Cell>
          <Table.Cell column="state">Draft</Table.Cell>
        </Table.Row>
        <Table.LoadMore loading onLoadMore={() => undefined}>
          Loading more items
        </Table.LoadMore>
      </Table.Body>
    </Table.Root>
  );
}

const VariableVirtualizationAndLoadingCompact = () => <VirtualizedTable />;

Places an application-owned action control in an ordinary keyed cell rather than teaching Table application commands or business workflows.
const ActionsStayWithApplications = () => (
  <Table.Root aria-label="Action table">
    <Table.Header>
      <Table.Column id="name" rowHeader>
        Name
      </Table.Column>
      <Table.Column id="action">Action</Table.Column>
    </Table.Header>
    <Table.Body>
      <Table.Row id="alpha" textValue="Alpha Inspect">
        <Table.Cell column="name">Alpha</Table.Cell>
        <Table.Cell column="action">
          <Button appearance="ghost" size="sm">
            Inspect
          </Button>
        </Table.Cell>
      </Table.Row>
    </Table.Body>
  </Table.Root>
);
Docs doc ok
./src/primitives/Table/Table.mdx
Responsive compound data table with ordered columns, keyed rows, selection, consumer-owned sorting, actions, grouped sections, loading, and optional virtualization.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Table.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Responsive compound data table with ordered columns, keyed rows, selection, consumer-owned sorting, actions, grouped sections, loading, and optional virtualization."
/>

# Table

`Table` presents comparable records in ordered columns while coordinating responsive labels, row focus, selection, sorting, actions, and large collections.

```tsx
import { Table } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use a table when people need to scan or compare the same attributes across records. Use [DescriptionList](?path=/docs/data-display-descriptionlist--docs) for the attributes of one record, [GridList](?path=/docs/collections-gridlist--docs) for rich selectable rows without aligned columns, and [List](?path=/docs/data-display-list--docs) for non-interactive sequences.

Avoid a table when each row has a different information structure or when a small-screen record cannot be understood from compact labels.

## Basic use

```tsx
import { Table } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function RecordsTable() {
  return (
    <Table.Root aria-label="Records">
      <Table.Header>
        <Table.Column id="name" rowHeader>
          Name
        </Table.Column>
        <Table.Column id="state">State</Table.Column>
      </Table.Header>
      <Table.Body>
        <Table.Row id="alpha" textValue="Alpha Ready">
          <Table.Cell column="name">Alpha</Table.Cell>
          <Table.Cell column="state">Ready</Table.Cell>
        </Table.Row>
        <Table.Row id="beta" textValue="Beta In review">
          <Table.Cell column="name">Beta</Table.Cell>
          <Table.Cell column="state">In review</Table.Cell>
        </Table.Row>
      </Table.Body>
    </Table.Root>
  );
}
```

<Canvas of={Stories.ControlledSortingAndSelection} />

## Anatomy

- `Table.Root` owns table semantics, layout, responsive labels, row state, sorting, and virtualization.
- `Table.Header` renders the ordered column-heading section from static children or generic `items`.
- `Table.Column` renders one keyed heading and configures row-header, sorting, compact label, alignment, and width behaviour.
- `Table.Body` renders one ordered row section and its empty content.
- `Table.Row` renders one keyed data or section row, with optional action and tone.
- `Table.Cell` associates one cell with a stable column key.
- `Table.Disclosure` renders the canonical bare arrow cell for an actionable row.
- `Table.Footer` renders an optional ordered footer row section.
- `Table.LoadMore` renders a visible loading row and intersection-driven request sentinel.

Keep `Header`, each `Body`, and optional `Footer` in meaningful source order. Every data row must contain exactly one cell for every visible column, in the same order, even after conditional column changes.

## Keys and collection content

Column, row, and section identifiers are stable `string | number` keys. Do not use array positions or translated labels. Each `Table.Cell column` must exactly match its `Table.Column id`. `Table.Row textValue` is required for typeahead and a plain-text accessible representation. Provide `Table.Column textValue` and `Table.Cell textValue` when visible content is not plain text; Breeze does not derive accessible or compact labels from arbitrary React nodes.

`Table.Header`, `Table.Body`, and `Table.Footer` accept either authored children or generic `items` plus a renderer. Dynamic items must contain a stable `id`. `id` on each section is optional but recommended for stable measurement when sections can reorder or virtualization is enabled.

## Responsive layouts and columns

`layout="responsive"` is the default. It presents compact labelled records below the Breeze small breakpoint and native table columns at wider sizes. `responsiveGrid` keeps the same compact treatment but uses explicit equal grid tracks at wider sizes. `grid` keeps CSS grid structure at all sizes. Use `responsiveGrid` or `grid` only when equal tracks and aligned custom geometry fit the content.

`Table.Column compactLabel` defaults to `true`; a literal string heading becomes the compact label. When the heading contains formatting, icons, or other React nodes, provide explicit `textValue`. Set `compactLabel` to `false` for a disclosure or control column that should not label compact cells. `align` is `start`, `center`, or `end` and defaults to `start`. `width` is `auto`, `control`, or `icon` and defaults to `auto`. `boundary="strong"` adds a stronger lower edge; `none` is the default.

<Canvas of={Stories.ResponsiveGroupedSections} />

## Selection, sorting, and actions

Selection is disabled until you supply selection state, a selection callback, or `multiple`. Uncontrolled selection uses `defaultSelection`; controlled selection requires `selection` and `onSelectionChange`; immutable selection requires `selection`, `readOnly`, and no selection callback. Values are `Array<string | number>` or `'all'`. `multiple` defaults to `false`, and `onSelectionChange` receives the complete next selection.

Sorting never sorts consumer data. Mark a column `sortable`, then either use `defaultSort` for retained uncontrolled sort metadata or pass controlled `sort` with `onSortChange`. A `TableSort` is `{ column: string | number, direction: 'ascending' | 'descending' }`. Reorder the supplied rows in response. `onSortChange` receives the next complete descriptor. Read-only mode suppresses sort changes as well as selection changes.

`Table.Row onAction` receives that row's stable key. Add `Table.Disclosure column="actions"` to an actionable row when a visual arrow is useful, and define a matching column with `compactLabel={false}` and `width="icon"`. `position="overlay"` is the disclosure default for compact records; `flow` keeps it in an explicit track. Buttons and links inside cells remain independent controls.

```tsx
import { useState } from 'react';
import {
  Table,
  type CollectionSelection,
  type TableSort,
} from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

export function ControlledTable() {
  const [selection, setSelection] = useState<CollectionSelection>([]);
  const [sort, setSort] = useState<TableSort>({
    column: 'name',
    direction: 'ascending',
  });

  return (
    <Table.Root
      aria-label="Records"
      onSelectionChange={setSelection}
      onSortChange={setSort}
      selection={selection}
      sort={sort}
    >
      <Table.Header>
        <Table.Column id="name" rowHeader sortable>
          Name
        </Table.Column>
      </Table.Header>
      <Table.Body emptyContent="No records">
        <Table.Row id="alpha" textValue="Alpha">
          <Table.Cell column="name">Alpha</Table.Cell>
        </Table.Row>
      </Table.Body>
    </Table.Root>
  );
}
```

## Row presentation and states

`Table.Row presentation="data"` and `tone="default"` are defaults. Use `tone="muted"` for domain-neutral de-emphasis, not disabled state. `presentation="section"` creates a grouped section row; it is structure inside an ordered body, not a substitute for column headings. `disabled` or `disabledKeys` prevent row focus, selection, and actions.

`Table.Body emptyContent` defaults to `No rows`; supply translated copy. `Table.LoadMore onLoadMore` requests more consumer-owned rows. Set `loading` for the duration of the request to show loading content and suppress duplicates; `offset` defaults to `1` viewport proportion. Table has no built-in error row: render application feedback and a real retry action without breaking the header/body structure.

## Virtualization

Pass `virtualization` only for a large collection. Both modes require `viewportHeight` in pixels.

| Mode                   | Required                      | Optional defaults and behaviour                                           |
| ---------------------- | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `{ mode: 'fixed' }`    | `rowHeight`, `viewportHeight` | One authoritative row height; `gap` defaults to `0`.                      |
| `{ mode: 'variable' }` | `viewportHeight`              | `estimatedRowHeight` defaults to `48`; live row measurements override it. |

Both modes accept `overscan` (default `0` beyond the engine buffer), `loadingRowHeight` (default `48`), and `estimatedSectionHeight`. Keep section and row keys stable. Test responsive content at the configured height; clipping a variable-height row by using fixed mode is a data-loss bug.

<Canvas of={Stories.VariableVirtualizationAndLoading} />

## Keyboard, accessibility, and internationalization

Every table needs an accessible name through visible context, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`. Mark the primary identifying column `rowHeader` so assistive technology can announce records while navigating cells. Arrow keys move through the table, Home and End move within row/collection boundaries, and typeahead uses row `textValue`. Standard selection keys follow the current mode; Enter or equivalent activation invokes actionable rows. Sortable headings are keyboard activatable. Embedded controls remain in the Tab sequence.

Translate the table name, headings, empty/loading/error copy, and text values. Format dates and numbers before rendering cells. `start`/`end`, logical disclosure positioning, and inherited direction adapt to RTL; keep semantic column and row source order stable rather than manually reversing it.

Parts support relevant native table attributes, `className`, ARIA and data attributes, events, and typed refs. Click, value, children, alignment, role, and inline-style props owned by the compound API are intentionally excluded where applicable.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes

- Do not mismatch cell `column` keys, heading order, or conditional visible columns.
- Do not assume `onSortChange` sorts rows for you.
- Do not use unstable row keys or omit `textValue`.
- Do not attach generic click handlers to rows; use `onAction` or cell controls.
- Do not use fixed virtualization for wrapping rows.
- Do not hide meaningful headings with `compactLabel={false}`.

## Related components

[GridList](?path=/docs/collections-gridlist--docs), [DescriptionList](?path=/docs/data-display-descriptionlist--docs), [List](?path=/docs/data-display-list--docs), and [Pagination](?path=/docs/patterns-navigation-pagination--docs).

import { Tabs } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Controlled Keyboard And Disabled story ok
controlled horizontal tabs with keyboard navigation
Keeps selection in application state while verifying horizontal arrow-key activation, matching panel visibility, and disabled-tab skipping.
function ControlledKeyboardAndDisabled() {
  const [value, setValue] = useState<CollectionKey>('profile');

  return (
    <Tabs.Root onChange={setValue} value={value}>
      <Tabs.List aria-label="Workspace settings">
        <Tabs.Tab id="profile">Profile</Tabs.Tab>
        <Tabs.Tab id={2}>Security</Tabs.Tab>
        <Tabs.Tab disabled id="advanced">
          Advanced
        </Tabs.Tab>
      </Tabs.List>
      <Tabs.Panels>
        <Tabs.Panel id="profile">Profile settings</Tabs.Panel>
        <Tabs.Panel id={2}>Security settings</Tabs.Panel>
        <Tabs.Panel id="advanced">Advanced settings</Tabs.Panel>
      </Tabs.Panels>
    </Tabs.Root>
  );
}
Vertical Read Only story ok
vertical read-only tab selection
Presents a vertical tab list whose controlled selection is intentionally immutable even when another enabled tab is clicked.
const VerticalReadOnly = () => (
  <Tabs.Root orientation="vertical" readOnly value={2}>
    <Tabs.List aria-label="Read only tabs">
      <Tabs.Tab id="one">One</Tabs.Tab>
      <Tabs.Tab id={2}>Two</Tabs.Tab>
    </Tabs.List>
    <Tabs.Panel id="one">One</Tabs.Panel>
    <Tabs.Panel id={2}>Two</Tabs.Panel>
  </Tabs.Root>
);

./src/primitives/Tabs/Tabs.mdx
Accessible keyed tab labels and associated panels with controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, and orientation-aware behaviour.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './Tabs.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Accessible keyed tab labels and associated panels with controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, disabled, and orientation-aware behaviour."
/>

# Tabs

`Tabs` switches between peer panels within the same page without changing the user's task context.

## Import

```tsx
import { Tabs } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use tabs for a small, stable set of related views whose labels fit comfortably. Use `NavigationList` when each destination is a route, `Accordion` when users benefit from reading several sections together, and `ToggleGroup` for compact display-mode choices.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Tabs } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<Tabs.Root defaultValue="summary">
  <Tabs.List aria-label="Record details">
    <Tabs.Tab id="summary">Summary</Tabs.Tab>
    <Tabs.Tab id="history">History</Tabs.Tab>
  </Tabs.List>
  <Tabs.Panels>
    <Tabs.Panel id="summary">Summary content</Tabs.Panel>
    <Tabs.Panel id="history">History content</Tabs.Panel>
  </Tabs.Panels>
</Tabs.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.ControlledKeyboardAndDisabled} />

## Anatomy

| Part          | Purpose                                                |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `Tabs.Root`   | Owns keyed selection, disabled state, and orientation. |
| `Tabs.List`   | Accessible list of tab labels.                         |
| `Tabs.Tab`    | One keyed selectable label.                            |
| `Tabs.Panels` | Optional wrapper coordinating panel transitions.       |
| `Tabs.Panel`  | Content whose `id` matches one `Tab`.                  |

Every tab and panel pair must use the same stable `string | number` id. `Panels` is optional, but useful when panels are grouped; do not place unrelated content between a tab and its associated panel structure.

## Selection, states, and orientation

Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled selection, `value` with `onChange` for controlled selection, or `value` with `readOnly` for immutable selection. `onChange` receives the complete next key, not an event. `disabled` on `Root` disables all tabs; `Tab.disabled` disables one and removes it from selection navigation. `forceMount` keeps an inactive panel mounted while React Aria preserves inactive semantics; use it only when local state or expensive setup must survive.

Horizontal orientation is the default and uses Left/Right keys. Vertical orientation uses Up/Down keys and places labels alongside panels. Tabs have no built-in loading, invalid, empty, or error state; keep a stable labelled tab set and put relevant feedback inside its panel.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Native attributes and styling

All parts accept `className`, relevant `aria-*` and `data-*` attributes, and a ref to their rendered `div`. Inline styles and native click/change handlers are excluded where Breeze owns behaviour.

## Accessibility, direction, and mistakes

Label `Tabs.List`. Tab moves into the active tab; arrow keys move and activate selection, Home and End reach the first and last enabled tab, and Tab then moves into panel content. React Aria mirrors horizontal arrow behaviour for RTL. Translate visible labels without changing ids and allow labels to wrap only when the resulting layout remains understandable.

Do not use tabs as route links, mismatch ids, hide critical errors in an inactive panel without an announcement, or create so many tabs that labels become an overflow menu. Related components: `NavigationList`, `Accordion`, `Disclosure`, and `ToggleGroup`.
List 2 prop types

 */

/**

 */

Coordinates tag selection, focus, and removal.
Prop types (react-docgen-typescript) 8 prop types 
Component: src/primitives/TagGroup/TagGroup.tsx::Root
Props:
/**
 * Compound label, tag list, and supporting guidance parts.
 */
children: ReactNode

/**
 * Excluded when selection is controlled.
 * Initial selected tag keys.
 */
defaultSelection?: CollectionSelection

/**
 * Keys whose tags cannot receive focus, selection, actions, or removal.
 */
disabledKeys?: Iterable<CollectionKey>

/**
 * Called with the stable keys removed through keyboard interaction.
 */
onRemove?: ((keys: CollectionKey[]) => void)

/**
 * Called with the next selected tag keys.
 * Excluded because read-only selection cannot change.
 */
onSelectionChange?: ((selection: CollectionSelection) => void) | ((selection: CollectionSelection) => void)

/**
 * Controlled mutable state cannot be marked read-only.
 * Prevents selection changes and removal.
 * Uncontrolled state cannot be marked read-only.
 */
readOnly?: boolean = false

/**
 * Ref to the rendered tag-group root.
 */
ref?: Ref<HTMLDivElement>

/**
 * Current selected tag keys, or every key for select-all state.
 * Current immutable selected tag keys.
 * Excluded when selection is uncontrolled.
 */
selection?: CollectionSelection
Imports 
import { TagGroup } from "@motech-development/breeze-ui";
Static Keyboard story ok
static selectable tags with keyboard semantics
Selects a statically authored keyed tag through the collection interaction model and exposes the resulting selected state semantically.
const StaticKeyboard = () => (
  <TagGroup.Root onSelectionChange={() => undefined}>
    <TagGroup.Label>Filters</TagGroup.Label>
    <TagGroup.List>
      <Tag id="open" textValue="Open">
        Open
      </Tag>
      <Tag id="paid" textValue="Paid">
        Paid
      </Tag>
    </TagGroup.List>
  </TagGroup.Root>
);
Items story ok
statically authored tag collection items
Authors tags directly with stable numeric identities and visible text used by the collection interaction model.
const Items = () => (
  <TagGroup.Root>
    <TagGroup.Label>Items</TagGroup.Label>
    <TagGroup.List>
      <Tag id={1} textValue="Open">
        Open
      </Tag>
      <Tag id={2} textValue="Paid">
        Paid
      </Tag>
    </TagGroup.List>
  </TagGroup.Root>
);
Controlled And Read Only story ok
controlled and read-only tag selections
Compares application-controlled multiple selection with an intentionally immutable selected collection using the same keyed item model.
const ControlledAndReadOnly = () => (
  <div>
    <Controlled />
    <TagGroup.Root readOnly selection={[1]}>
      <TagGroup.Label>Read only</TagGroup.Label>
      <TagGroup.List>
        <Tag id={1} textValue="Open">
          Open
        </Tag>
        <Tag id={2} textValue="Paid">
          Paid
        </Tag>
      </TagGroup.List>
    </TagGroup.Root>
  </div>
);
Empty Disabled story ok
empty tag list with disabled-key configuration
Demonstrates application-owned empty content while retaining the root-level disabled-key contract used when items are later supplied.
const EmptyDisabled = () => (
  <TagGroup.Root disabledKeys={[2]}>
    <TagGroup.Label>States</TagGroup.Label>
    <TagGroup.List emptyContent="No tags">{null}</TagGroup.List>
  </TagGroup.Root>
);
Extreme story ok
long tag content in a narrow collection
Wraps a long selected-value label inside a narrow collection host to verify that the tag group remains readable without horizontal overflow.
const Extreme = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <TagGroup.Root>
      <TagGroup.Label>Long labels</TagGroup.Label>
      <TagGroup.List>
        <Tag id="long" textValue="Long">
          A very long tag that wraps
        </Tag>
      </TagGroup.List>
    </TagGroup.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);

./src/primitives/TagGroup/TagGroup.mdx
Labelled static or typed tag collection with optional multiple selection, disabled keys, controlled, uncontrolled and read-only state, application-owned removal, and an explicit empty state.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './TagGroup.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Labelled static or typed tag collection with optional multiple selection, disabled keys, controlled, uncontrolled and read-only state, application-owned removal, and an explicit empty state."
/>

# TagGroup

`TagGroup` coordinates a labelled collection of keyed `Tag` items with optional multiple selection and application-owned removal.

## Import

```tsx
import { Tag, TagGroup } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for compact chosen filters, categories, recipients, or similar items where selection or removal is meaningful. Prefer `Badge` plus a layout primitive for passive metadata, `CheckboxGroup` for form choices with persistent checkbox affordances, and `ListBox` for a larger selectable collection. Do not use tags as a substitute for navigation links.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { Tag, TagGroup } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<TagGroup.Root onRemove={(keys) => removeTopics(keys)}>
  <TagGroup.Label>Topics</TagGroup.Label>
  <TagGroup.List>
    <Tag id="design" textValue="Design">
      Design
    </Tag>
    <Tag id="research" textValue="Research">
      Research
    </Tag>
  </TagGroup.List>
  <TagGroup.Description>Delete removes the focused topic.</TagGroup.Description>
</TagGroup.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.StaticKeyboard} />

## Anatomy

| Part                   | Purpose                                                                                                   |
| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `TagGroup.Root`        | Owns collection focus, optional multiple selection, disabled keys, read-only state, and removal callback. |
| `TagGroup.Label`       | Persistent accessible name for the tag collection.                                                        |
| `TagGroup.List`        | Renders static or typed generic tags and the empty state.                                                 |
| `Tag`                  | One keyed item with plain-text representation, disabled state, and optional action.                       |
| `TagGroup.Description` | Supporting guidance associated with the collection.                                                       |

There is no group error part or invalid/required API. Validate the application concept in surrounding form composition if necessary.

## Collections, selection, and removal

Static lists contain authored `Tag` children. Dynamic lists accept `items`, each with stable `id: string | number`, and a child renderer.

```tsx
<TagGroup.List items={topics}>
  {(topic) => (
    <Tag id={topic.id} textValue={topic.name}>
      {topic.name}
    </Tag>
  )}
</TagGroup.List>
```

Selection is opt-in. Supplying `defaultSelection`, `selection`, or `onSelectionChange` enables multiple selection. Values use `CollectionKey[] | 'all'`. Use `defaultSelection` for uncontrolled state, `selection` with `onSelectionChange` for controlled state, or `selection` with `readOnly` for immutable state. The callback receives the complete next selection.

`onRemove` receives all stable keys removed through keyboard interaction. It does not mutate consumer data; remove the matching items in application state. It is unavailable in read-only mode. `disabledKeys` prevents matching tags from focus, selection, action, and removal. `emptyContent` defaults to `'No tags'`. Loading and loading errors are application-owned.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and direction

- Tab enters and leaves the tag collection; arrow keys move between enabled tags.
- Selection keys follow tag-group conventions when selection is enabled. Delete or Backspace requests removal when `onRemove` is supplied.
- Disabled tags are skipped. Read-only mode prevents selection changes and removal.
- Provide `Label`, an accurate `textValue` for every Tag, and `Description` when removal or selection is not obvious.
- Keep keys locale-independent. Translated labels, descriptions, and empty content may change without changing selection.
- Wrapping and arrow interpretation follow surrounding direction.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not assume `onRemove` changes the items, enable selection accidentally with an unused callback, use translated labels as keys, omit `textValue`, or put buttons inside tags. Use `Badge` for passive metadata, `CheckboxGroup` for form choices, and `ListBox` for a larger or virtualised collection.

/**
 * Content displayed when the tag collection has no items.
 */

/**
 * Generic items with stable `id` keys.
 * Excluded when static compound item elements are supplied.
 */

/**
 * Ref to the rendered tag-list element.
 */

Anatomy And Uncontrolled story ok
uncontrolled multiline entry with complete field anatomy
Composes the full labelled multiline anatomy and verifies native typing plus line breaks in an internally managed textarea value.
const AnatomyAndUncontrolled = () => (
  <TextArea.Root>
    <TextArea.Label>Notes</TextArea.Label>
    <TextArea.Control name="notes" placeholder="Add context" rows={5} />
    <TextArea.Description>Use plain text.</TextArea.Description>
  </TextArea.Root>
);
Controlled story ok
application-controlled multiline value and character count
Stores multiline content in application state and reflects the current character count through associated supporting guidance.
const Controlled = () => <ControlledExample />;
Read Only And States story ok
read-only disabled and invalid multiline field states
Compares immutable, disabled, and required-invalid textareas across the supported control sizes so their distinct semantics remain visible.
const ReadOnlyAndStates = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="bounded">
    <Stack gap="lg">
      <TextArea.Root readOnly value="Archived content">
        <TextArea.Label>Read-only</TextArea.Label>
        <TextArea.Control />
      </TextArea.Root>
      <TextArea.Root disabled defaultValue="Unavailable">
        <TextArea.Label>Disabled</TextArea.Label>
        <TextArea.Control size="sm" />
      </TextArea.Root>
      <TextArea.Root invalid required>
        <TextArea.Label>Required summary</TextArea.Label>
        <TextArea.Control size="lg" />
        <TextArea.Error>Enter a summary.</TextArea.Error>
      </TextArea.Root>
    </Stack>
  </StoryConstraint>
);
Keyboard And A 11 Y story ok
keyboard focus and native multiline editing behavior
Verifies Tab reaches the labelled textarea while documenting that native Enter and text-editing behavior remain available inside the control.
const KeyboardAndA11y = () => (
  <TextArea.Root>
    <TextArea.Label>Keyboard notes</TextArea.Label>
    <TextArea.Control />
    <TextArea.Description>
      Tab enters and leaves the control; Enter inserts a new line.
    </TextArea.Description>
  </TextArea.Root>
);
Content Extreme story ok
long multiline content constrained to a narrow field
Places a long wrapping value, label, and description in a narrow host to demonstrate scrolling and user-controlled vertical resizing.
const ContentExtreme = () => (
  <StoryConstraint size="narrow-control">
    <TextArea.Root
      defaultValue={'A long paragraph demonstrates wrapping. '.repeat(12)}
    >
      <TextArea.Label>A detailed multiline content label</TextArea.Label>
      <TextArea.Control rows={10} />
      <TextArea.Description>
        Long content remains scrollable and the control remains resizable.
      </TextArea.Description>
    </TextArea.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);

./src/primitives/TextArea/TextArea.mdx
Labelled multiline text entry with controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, validation, sizing, and native textarea behaviour.
import { ArgTypes, Canvas, Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
import * as Stories from './TextArea.stories';

<Meta
  of={Stories}
  summary="Labelled multiline text entry with controlled, uncontrolled, read-only, validation, sizing, and native textarea behaviour."
/>

# TextArea

`TextArea` collects free-form content that can span several lines while keeping label, guidance, and validation explicitly composed.

## Import

```tsx
import { TextArea } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';
```

## When to use

Use it for notes, summaries, descriptions, and other paragraph-length content. Prefer `TextField` for a short single-line value. Do not use a textarea for structured rich text, code editing, or a choice from known values; use a purpose-built editor or selection control.

## Basic example

```tsx
import { TextArea } from '@motech-development/breeze-ui';

<TextArea.Root>
  <TextArea.Label>Notes</TextArea.Label>
  <TextArea.Control name="notes" rows={5} />
  <TextArea.Description>Use plain text.</TextArea.Description>
</TextArea.Root>;
```

<Canvas of={Stories.AnatomyAndUncontrolled} />

## Anatomy

| Part                   | Purpose                                                  |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| `TextArea.Root`        | Owns string value and shared field state.                |
| `TextArea.Label`       | Persistently names the textarea.                         |
| `TextArea.Control`     | Renders the native multiline textarea.                   |
| `TextArea.Description` | Associates supporting guidance with the control.         |
| `TextArea.Error`       | Associates validation feedback when the root is invalid. |

All parts belong inside `Root`. `TextArea` intentionally shares its state and guidance contract with `TextField`; the editable part is named `Control` rather than `Input`.

## Value and states

Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled state, `value` with `onChange` for controlled state, or `value` with `readOnly` for an intentionally immutable value. `onChange` receives the complete next string. Empty content is `''`.

`disabled` prevents focus and edits. `readOnly` preserves focus, selection, copying, and form participation without allowing edits. `invalid` associates `Error`; `required` exposes native required semantics. Loading and character limits are application concerns: use native `maxLength` where it is a genuine constraint and keep any count in `Description`.

## Size and resizing

`Control` supports `size="sm" | "md" | "lg"`; `md` is the default. Its minimum height changes with size and users may resize it vertically. Use `rows` to express the expected amount of content, but do not force a fixed height that prevents reviewing entered text.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and direction

- Tab moves focus; Enter inserts a new line. Normal platform editing, selection, clipboard, and undo commands remain available.
- Always provide a persistent `Label`. A placeholder is a hint, not an accessible name.
- Put pre-entry guidance in `Description`; put a specific recovery instruction in `Error` and set `invalid`.
- Do not intercept Enter to submit the surrounding form unless the product clearly provides another way to enter line breaks.
- Unicode text and the surrounding `dir` are respected. Use `dir="auto"` for user-authored text whose direction is unknown.

## API

<ArgTypes />

## Common mistakes and related components

Do not put state props on `Control`, remove resize access without an equivalent, or use the placeholder as the only label. Use `TextField` for a line, `SearchField` for a query, and `FormSection` or `Fieldset` to organise multiple related fields.
Control 3 prop types

 * Placement and composition classes.
 */

 */

/**
 * Canonical control size. Defaults to `md`.
 */
size?: ControlSize

        <TextField.Label>Read-only</TextField.Label>
        <TextField.Input />

      <TextField.Root disabled defaultValue="Unavailable">
        <TextField.Label>Disabled</TextField.Label>
        <TextField.Input size="sm" />
      </TextField.Root>
      <TextField.Root invalid required>
        <TextField.Label>Required reference</TextField.Label>
        <TextField.Input size="lg" />
        <TextField.Error>Enter a reference.</TextField.Error>
      </TextField.Root>

keyboard focus with native form field integration

const KeyboardAndNativeForm = () => (
  <form onSubmit={(event) => event.preventDefault()}>
    <TextField.Root defaultValue="BRZ-3" required>
      <TextField.Label>Project code</TextField.Label>
      <TextField.Input autoComplete="off" name="projectCode" />
      <TextField.Description>
        Submitted through the native form.
      </TextField.Description>
    </TextField.Root>
  </form>
);

      <TextField.Label>
        A persistent label with unusually detailed wording
      </TextField.Label>
      <TextField.Input />
      <TextField.Description>
        Long guidance wraps without changing the field&apos;s accessible
        relationships.
      </TextField.Description>
    </TextField.Root>
  </StoryConstraint>
);
